messages.content: For 6200 AOS-CX 10.06, what does the guide say about Access Control Lists (ACLs)?

Access Control Lists (ACLs): About Access Control Lists (ACLs) Access Control Lists (ACLs) let a network administrator permit or deny passage of traffic based on network addresses, protocols, service ports, and other packet attributes. ACLs are composed of one or more Access Control Entries (called ACEs). Each ACE defines a filter criteria and an action, either permit or deny. If the traffic matches the filter criteria, the specified action is taken. The permit action permits the traffic to continue through the switch. The deny action causes the traffic to be discarded (dropped). ACEs can also log or count matching traffic. Three ACL types are supported; IPv4, IPv6, and MAC. Each ACL type is focused on relevant frame or packet characteristics. ACLs must be applied (using an apply access-list command) to take effect. ACLs can be applied to interfaces (including LAGs), VLANs, or the Control Plane. Access Control Entries (ACEs) are listed according to priority by sequence number and processed in lowest to highest sequence number order.
For 6200 AOS-CX 10.06, what does the access-list copy command do?
The access-list copy command copies an IPv4, IPv6, or MAC ACL to a new destination ACL or overwrites an existing ACL. Syntax: access-list {ip|IPv6|mac} <ACL-NAME> copy <DESTINATION-ACL>.
For 6200 AOS-CX 10.06, what is the syntax of the access-list copy command?
Syntax: access-list {ip|IPv6|mac} <ACL-NAME> copy <DESTINATION-ACL>
For 6200 AOS-CX 10.06, what does the access-list log-timer command do?
The access-list log-timer command sets the log timer interval for all ACEs that have the log parameter configured. Syntax: access-list log-timer {default|<INTERVAL>}.
For 6200 AOS-CX 10.06, what is the syntax of the access-list log-timer command?
Syntax: access-list log-timer {default|<INTERVAL>}
For 6200 AOS-CX 10.06, what does the access-list resequence command do?
The access-list resequence command resequences the ACE sequence numbers in an ACL. Syntax: access-list {ip|IPv6|mac} <ACL-NAME> resequence <STARTING-SEQUENCE-NUMBER> <INCREMENT>.
For 6200 AOS-CX 10.06, what is the syntax of the access-list resequence command?
Syntax: access-list {ip|IPv6|mac} <ACL-NAME> resequence <STARTING-SEQUENCE-NUMBER> <INCREMENT>
For 6200 AOS-CX 10.06, what is the syntax of the access-list reset command?
Syntax: access-list {all|ip <ACL-NAME>|IPv6 <ACL-NAME>|mac <ACL-NAME>} reset
For 6200 AOS-CX 10.06, what does the apply access-list control-plane command do?

The apply access-list control-plane command applies an ACL to the specified VRF. The no form of this command removes application of the ACL from the specified VRF. Syntax: apply access-list {ip|IPv6} <ACL-NAME> control-plane vrf <VRF-NAME>.
For 6200 AOS-CX 10.06, what is the syntax of the apply access-list control-plane command?
Syntax: apply access-list {ip|IPv6} <ACL-NAME> control-plane vrf <VRF-NAME>
For 6200 AOS-CX 10.06, what does the apply access-list (to interface or LAG) command do?

The apply access-list (to interface or LAG) command applies an ACL to the interface (Individual front plane port) or Link Aggregation Group (LAG) identified by the current interface or LAG context. The no form of this command removes application of the ACL from the current interface or LAG identified by the current interface or LAG context. Syntax: apply access-list {ip|IPv6|mac} <ACL-NAME> {in|out}.
For 6200 AOS-CX 10.06, what is the syntax of the apply access-list (to interface or LAG) command?
Syntax: apply access-list {ip|IPv6|mac} <ACL-NAME> {in|out}
For 6200 AOS-CX 10.06, what does the apply access-list (to interface VLAN) command do?

The apply access-list (to interface VLAN) command applies an ACL to the interface VLAN (or range of interface VLANs) identified by the current interface VLAN context. Using the apply access-list command on an interface VLAN interface with an already-applied ACL of the same direction and type will replace the currently-applied ACL. The no form of this command removes application of the ACL from the interface VLAN (or range of interface VLANs) identified by the current interface VLAN context. Syntax: apply access-list {ip|IPv6} <ACL-NAME> {routed-in|routed-out}.
For 6200 AOS-CX 10.06, what is the syntax of the apply access-list (to interface VLAN) command?
Syntax: apply access-list {ip|IPv6} <ACL-NAME> {routed-in|routed-out}
For 6200 AOS-CX 10.06, what does the apply access-list (to VLAN) command do?

The apply access-list (to VLAN) command applies an ACL to the VLAN identified by the current VLAN context. The no form of this command removes application of the ACL from the VLAN identified by the current VLAN context. Syntax: apply access-list {ip|IPv6|mac} <ACL-NAME> {in|out}.
For 6200 AOS-CX 10.06, what is the syntax of the apply access-list (to VLAN) command?
Syntax: apply access-list {ip|IPv6|mac} <ACL-NAME> {in|out}
For 6200 AOS-CX 10.06, what does the clear access-list hitcounts control-plane command do?

The clear access-list hitcounts control-plane command clears the hit counts for ACLs applied to the specified Control Plane VRF. Syntax: clear access-list hitcounts [{ip|IPv6} <ACL-NAME>] control-plane vrf <VRF-NAME>.
For 6200 AOS-CX 10.06, what is the syntax of the clear access-list hitcounts control-plane command?
Syntax: clear access-list hitcounts [{ip|IPv6} <ACL-NAME>] control-plane vrf <VRF-NAME>
For 6200 AOS-CX 10.06, what is the syntax of the object-group {ip|IPv6} address <OBJECT-GROUP-NAME> resequence <STARTING-SEQUENCE-NUMBER> <INCREMENT> command?
Syntax: object-group {ip|IPv6} address <OBJECT-GROUP-NAME> resequence <STARTING-SEQUENCE-NUMBER> <INCREMENT>
For 6200 AOS-CX 10.06, what does the show access-list control-plane command do?

The show access-list control-plane command shows information about your defined ACLs that have been applied to the Control Plane. When show access-list control-plane is entered without parameters, information for all ACLs applied to the Control Plane is shown. The parameters filter the list of ACLs for which information is shown. Available filtering includes: • The content of a specific ACL that has been applied to the Control Plane. • All ACLs of a specific type that have been applied to the Control Plane. • All ACLs applied to the Control Plane for a specific VRF. Syntax: show access-list [ip|IPv6] [<ACL-NAME>] control-plane [vrf <VRF-NAME>].
For 6200 AOS-CX 10.06, what is the syntax of the show access-list control-plane command?
Syntax: show access-list [ip|IPv6] [<ACL-NAME>] control-plane [vrf <VRF-NAME>]
For 6200 AOS-CX 10.06, what does the show access-list hitcounts control-plane command do?

The show access-list hitcounts control-plane command shows the hit count of the number of times an ACL (applied to the Control Plane) has matched a packet for ACEs with the count keyword. For ACEs without the count keyword, a dash is shown in place of a hit count. Syntax: show access-list hitcounts [{ip|IPv6} <ACL-NAME>] control-plane vrf <VRF-NAME> [vsx-peer].
For 6200 AOS-CX 10.06, what is the syntax of the show access-list hitcounts control-plane command?
Syntax: show access-list hitcounts [{ip|IPv6} <ACL-NAME>] control-plane vrf <VRF-NAME> [vsx-peer]
For 6200 AOS-CX 10.06, what does the show capacities command do?
The show capacities command shows system capacities and their values for all features or a specific feature. Syntax: show capacities <FEATURE> [vsx-peer].
For 6200 AOS-CX 10.06, what is the syntax of the show capacities command?
Syntax: show capacities <FEATURE> [vsx-peer]
For 6200 AOS-CX 10.06, what does the show capacities-status command do?
The show capacities-status command shows system capacities status and their values for all features or a specific feature. Syntax: show capacities-status <FEATURE>.
For 6200 AOS-CX 10.06, what is the syntax of the show capacities-status command?
Syntax: show capacities-status <FEATURE>
For 6200 AOS-CX 10.06, what does the guide say about Classifier policies?

Classifier policies: overview Classifier policies let a network administrator define sets of rules based on network traffic addressing or other header content, and use these rules to restrict or alter the passage of traffic through the switch. Choosing the rule criteria is called Classification, and one such rule, or list, is called a policy. Classification is achieved by creating a traffic class. The three types of classes (MAC, IPv4, and IPv6) are each focused on relevant frame/packet characteristics. Classes can be configured to match or ignore almost any frame or packet header field. Network traffic passing through a switch can be classified based on many different frame/packet characteristics including, but not limited to: •.
For 6200 AOS-CX 10.06, what does the apply policy command do?

The apply policy command applies a policy to the global config context. Only one policy can be globally applied at a time. Applying a policy globally again, replaces the previous globally applied policy. The no form of this command removes application of the global policy. Syntax: apply policy <POLICY-NAME> in.
For 6200 AOS-CX 10.06, what is the syntax of the apply policy command?
Syntax: apply policy <POLICY-NAME> in
For 6200 AOS-CX 10.06, what does the class copy command do?

The class copy command copies a IPv4, IPv6, or MAC class to a new destination class or overwrites an existing class. Copying a class copies all entries as well. Syntax: class {ip|IPv6|mac} <CLASS-NAME> copy <DESTINATION-CLASS>.
For 6200 AOS-CX 10.06, what is the syntax of the class copy command?
Syntax: class {ip|IPv6|mac} <CLASS-NAME> copy <DESTINATION-CLASS>
For 6200 AOS-CX 10.06, what does the class resequence command do?
The class resequence command resequence numbering in an IPv4, IPv6, or MAC class. Syntax: class {ip|IPv6|mac} <CLASS-NAME> resequence <STARTING-SEQUENCE-NUMBER> <INCREMENT>.
For 6200 AOS-CX 10.06, what is the syntax of the class resequence command?
Syntax: class {ip|IPv6|mac} <CLASS-NAME> resequence <STARTING-SEQUENCE-NUMBER> <INCREMENT>
For 6200 AOS-CX 10.06, what is the syntax of the class reset command?
Syntax: class { all | ip <CLASS-NAME> | IPv6 <CLASS-NAME> |mac <CLASS-NAME> } reset
For 6200 AOS-CX 10.06, what does the policy command do?

The policy command creates or modifies classifier policy and policy entries. A policy is made up of one or more policy entries ordered and prioritized by sequence numbers. Each entry has an IPv4/IPv6/MAC class and zero or more policy actions associated with it. A policy must be applied using the apply command. The no form of the command can be used to delete either a policy (use no with the policy command) or an individual policy entry (use no with the sequence number). Syntax: policy <POLICY-NAME>.
For 6200 AOS-CX 10.06, what is the syntax of the policy command?
Syntax: policy <POLICY-NAME>
For 6200 AOS-CX 10.06, what does the policy copy command do?

The policy copy command copies a policy to a new destination policy or overwrites an existing policy. Copying a policy copies all its entries as well. Syntax: policy <POLICY-NAME> copy <DESTINATION-POLICY>.
For 6200 AOS-CX 10.06, what is the syntax of the policy copy command?
Syntax: policy <POLICY-NAME> copy <DESTINATION-POLICY>
For 6200 AOS-CX 10.06, what does the policy resequence command do?
The policy resequence command resequences numbering in a policy. Syntax: policy <POLICY-NAME> resequence <STARTING-SEQ-NUM> <INCREMENT>.
For 6200 AOS-CX 10.06, what is the syntax of the policy resequence command?
Syntax: policy <POLICY-NAME> resequence <STARTING-SEQ-NUM> <INCREMENT>
For 6200 AOS-CX 10.06, what does the policy reset command do?

The policy reset command changes the user-specified policy configuration to match the active policy configuration. Use this command when a discrepancy exists between what the user configured and what is active and accepted by the system. Syntax: policy <POLICY-NAME> reset.
For 6200 AOS-CX 10.06, what is the syntax of the policy reset command?
Syntax: policy <POLICY-NAME> reset
For 6200 AOS-CX 10.06, what does the guide say about Port Access Client Policy?

Port Access Client Policy: This feature is not classifier related but uses one lookup each from the above group of features: Ingress L2 Tunnel There are four TCAM lookups available for these features. Each of these features uses one TCAM lookup when enabled. At most, four of these features can be enabled at the same time. Egress Port IP ACL Egress Port MAC ACL Egress Port Policy Egress VLAN IP ACL Egress Routed VLAN IP ACL Egress VLAN MAC ACL.
For 6200 AOS-CX 10.06, what does the guide say about Egress VLAN Policy?

Egress VLAN Policy: Matching precedence order When a packet is matched by multiple TCAM Lookups with the same action, a precedence order is followed. For example, if a packet matches an IPv6 ACL with a count action and a MAC ACL with a count action, the IPv6 count action takes precedence and the MAC ACL will not count the packet. However, if a packet matches both an ACL and a policy with count actions, both will be counted. Regardless of precedence, if a.
For 6200 AOS-CX 10.06, what does the guide say about Ingress Global Policy?

Ingress Global Policy: QoS Actions: Port Access Client Policy Remark Ingress/Egress Port Policy Remark Ingress/Egress VLAN Policy Remark Global Policy Remark QoS DSCP Map Entry QoS COS Map Entry QoS Port Config MAC Port ACL Logging IP Port ACL Logging MAC VLAN ACL Logging.
For 6200 AOS-CX 10.06, what does the guide say about Port Access Client Policy Captive-portal?

Port Access Client Policy Captive-portal: L4 port ranges Any ACE or class entry that uses 'lt', 'gt', 'range', or port groups may use more than one hardware entry to represent the range of L4 ports. Context group selectors Context group selectors are a limited hardware resource that are required for applying ACLs and classifier policies. The selectors enable the application of an ACL or classifier policy to multiple instances of the same context (for example, ports on a line card or VLANs) without consuming additional resources. There are a limited number of available context group selectors for each context group (Ingress Ports, Ingress VLANs, Egress Ports, Egress VLANs). NOTE: IP ACLs require two selectors that are allocated together; one selector for each address family (IPv4 and IPv6). Context group selectors work on a first-come-first-served basis. IP ACLs and Classes require two selectors that are allocated together; one selector for each address family (IPv4 and IPv6).
For 6200 AOS-CX 10.06, what does the show resources command do?

The show resources command shows hardware resource consumption for the specified VSF member or for all VSF members. Resource data is updated every 10 seconds. Hardware resource consumption information is shown for: • TCAM entries • TCAM lookups • Policers. Syntax: show resources [<SLOT-ID>] [vsx-peer].
For 6200 AOS-CX 10.06, what is the syntax of the show resources command?
Syntax: show resources [<SLOT-ID>] [vsx-peer]
For 6200 AOS-CX 10.06, what does the access-list mac <ACL-NAME> command do?
The access-list mac <ACL-NAME> command is documented in this guide. Syntax: no access-list mac <ACL-NAME>.
For 6200 AOS-CX 10.06, what is the syntax of the access-list mac <ACL-NAME> command?
Syntax: no access-list mac <ACL-NAME>
For 6200 AOS-CX 10.06, what does the IPv6 neighbor <IPv6-ADDR> mac <MAC-ADDR> command do?

The IPv6 neighbor <IPv6-ADDR> mac <MAC-ADDR> command specifies a permanent static neighbor entry in the ARP table (for IPv6 neighbors). The no form of this command deletes a permanent static neighbor entry from the ARP table. Syntax: no IPv6 neighbor <IPv6-ADDR> mac <MAC-ADDR>.
For 6200 AOS-CX 10.06, what is the syntax of the IPv6 neighbor <IPv6-ADDR> mac <MAC-ADDR> command?
Syntax: no IPv6 neighbor <IPv6-ADDR> mac <MAC-ADDR>
For 6200 AOS-CX 10.06, what does the no shutdown command do?
The no shutdown command is documented in this guide. Syntax: no shutdown.
For 6200 AOS-CX 10.06, what is the syntax of the no shutdown command?
Syntax: no shutdown
For 6200 AOS-CX 10.06, what does the checkpoint post-configuration command do?

The checkpoint post-configuration command enables creation of system generated checkpoints when configuration changes occur. This feature is enabled by default. The no form of this command disables system generated checkpoints. Syntax: no checkpoint post-configuration.
For 6200 AOS-CX 10.06, what is the syntax of the checkpoint post-configuration command?
Syntax: no checkpoint post-configuration
For 6200 AOS-CX 10.06, what does the show debug buffer command do?

The show debug buffer command displays VSF member debug logs stored in the debug buffer, with an option to filter by VSF member and role. Displays the configured debug destination and severity. Syntax: show debug buffer vsf [member <MEMBER-ID>] [{master | standby}].
For 6200 AOS-CX 10.06, what is the syntax of the show debug buffer command?
Syntax: show debug buffer vsf [member <MEMBER-ID>] [{master | standby}]
For 6200 AOS-CX 10.06, what does the aaa authentication port-access allow-cdp-bpdu command do?

The aaa authentication port-access allow-cdp-bpdu command allows all packets related to the CDP (Cisco Discovery Protocol) BPDU (Bridge Protocol Data Unit) on a secure port. The no form of this command blocks the CDP BPDU on a secure port. On a nonsecure port, the command has no effect. Syntax: no aaa authentication port-access allow-cdp-bpdu.
For 6200 AOS-CX 10.06, what is the syntax of the aaa authentication port-access allow-cdp-bpdu command?
Syntax: no aaa authentication port-access allow-cdp-bpdu
For 6200 AOS-CX 10.06, what does the ip dns domain-list <DOMAIN-NAME> [vrf <VRF-NAME>] command do?

The ip dns domain-list <DOMAIN-NAME> [vrf <VRF-NAME>] command configures one or more domain names that are appended to the DNS request. The DNS client appends each name in succession until the DNS server replies. Domains can be either IPv4 or IPv6. By default, requests are forwarded on the default VRF. The no form of this command removes a domain from the list. Syntax: no ip dns domain-list <DOMAIN-NAME> [vrf <VRF-NAME>].
For 6200 AOS-CX 10.06, what is the syntax of the ip dns domain-list <DOMAIN-NAME> [vrf <VRF-NAME>] command?
Syntax: no ip dns domain-list <DOMAIN-NAME> [vrf <VRF-NAME>]
For 6200 AOS-CX 10.06, what does the ip dns domain-name <DOMAIN-NAME> [ vrf <VRF-NAME> ] command do?

The ip dns domain-name <DOMAIN-NAME> [ vrf <VRF-NAME> ] command configures a domain name that is appended to the DNS request. The domain can be either IPv4 or IPv6. By default, requests are forwarded on the default VRF. If a domain list is defined with the command ip dns domain-list, the domain name defined with this command is ignored. The no form of this command removes the domain name. Syntax: no ip dns domain-name <DOMAIN-NAME> [ vrf <VRF-NAME> ].
For 6200 AOS-CX 10.06, what is the syntax of the ip dns domain-name <DOMAIN-NAME> [ vrf <VRF-NAME> ] command?
Syntax: no ip dns domain-name <DOMAIN-NAME> [ vrf <VRF-NAME> ]
For 6200 AOS-CX 10.06, what does the ip dns host <HOST-NAME> <IP-ADDR> [ vrf <VRF-NAME> ] command do?

The ip dns host <HOST-NAME> <IP-ADDR> [ vrf <VRF-NAME> ] command associates a static IP address with a hostname. The DNS client returns this IP address instead of querying a DNS server for an IP address for the hostname. Up to six hosts can be defined. If no VRF is defined, the default VRF is used. The no form of this command removes a static IP address associated with a hostname. Syntax: no ip dns host <HOST-NAME> <IP-ADDR> [ vrf <VRF-NAME> ].
For 6200 AOS-CX 10.06, what is the syntax of the ip dns host <HOST-NAME> <IP-ADDR> [ vrf <VRF-NAME> ] command?
Syntax: no ip dns host <HOST-NAME> <IP-ADDR> [ vrf <VRF-NAME> ]
For 6200 AOS-CX 10.06, what does the erps ring <ringid> command do?

The erps ring <ringid> command this command creates an ERPS ring with a given ID. The no form of this command removes all the configurations of the ring, including instances. This command configures the ERPS ring member port. An L2 interface in the switch is associated to one of the two member ports of an ERPS ring. In case of an interconnection node, only port0 is applicable for the sub-ring. The no form of this command removes the association of the ring port to the L2 interface on the switch. This command adds descriptive information to help administrators and operators understand the purpose of a ring. 1-64 printable ASCII characters are allowed. The no form of this command removes the ring instance description. Syntax: erps ring <ringid>.
For 6200 AOS-CX 10.06, what is the syntax of the erps ring <ringid> command?
Syntax: erps ring <ringid>
For 6200 AOS-CX 10.06, what does the apply fault-monitor profile <profile-name> command do?

The apply fault-monitor profile <profile-name> command configures the fault monitoring profile to an interface. The profile is allowed to be modified after it is applied on a port. The no form of this command removes the fault monitoring profile from an interface. Syntax: no apply fault-monitor profile <profile-name>.
For 6200 AOS-CX 10.06, what is the syntax of the apply fault-monitor profile <profile-name> command?
Syntax: no apply fault-monitor profile <profile-name>
For 6200 AOS-CX 10.06, what does the vsx-sync command do?
The vsx-sync command configures VSX synchronization for a fault monitoring profile. The no form of this command removes the VSX synchronization for a fault monitoring profile. Syntax: no vsx-sync.
For 6200 AOS-CX 10.06, what is the syntax of the vsx-sync command?
Syntax: no vsx-sync
For 6200 AOS-CX 10.06, what does the ip igmp apply access-list <ACL-NAME> command do?

The ip igmp apply access-list <ACL-NAME> command configures the ACL on a particular interface to filter the IGMP join or leave packets based on rules set in the particular ACL name. The no form of this command unconfigures the rules set for the ACL. NOTE: This configuration will override the ACL associated with IGMP snooping on the corresponding L2 VLAN. Syntax: no ip igmp apply access-list <ACL-NAME>.
For 6200 AOS-CX 10.06, what is the syntax of the ip igmp apply access-list <ACL-NAME> command?
Syntax: no ip igmp apply access-list <ACL-NAME>
For 6200 AOS-CX 10.06, what does the ip igmp last-member-query-interval <INTERVAL-VALUE> command do?

The ip igmp last-member-query-interval <INTERVAL-VALUE> command configures an IGMP last member query interval value in seconds on an interface, depending on the command context you are in. The no form of this command sets the value to a default of 1 second on an interface. Chapter 33 IGMP commands 379. Syntax: no ip igmp last-member-query-interval <INTERVAL-VALUE>.
For 6200 AOS-CX 10.06, what is the syntax of the ip igmp last-member-query-interval <INTERVAL-VALUE> command?
Syntax: no ip igmp last-member-query-interval <INTERVAL-VALUE>
For 6200 AOS-CX 10.06, what does the ip igmp querier command do?

The ip igmp querier command configures an IGMP querier on an interface, depending on the command context you are in. This functionality will allow an interface to join in the querier-election process. The no form of this command disables IGMP querier on an interface. Configures the interval between IGMP queries on an interface, depending on the command context you are in. The no form of this command sets the IGMP querier interval to the default value of 125 seconds on an interface. Syntax: ip igmp querier interval <INTERVAL-VALUE>.
For 6200 AOS-CX 10.06, what is the syntax of the ip igmp querier command?
Syntax: ip igmp querier interval <INTERVAL-VALUE>
For 6200 AOS-CX 10.06, what does the ip igmp querier query-max-response-time <RESPONSE-TIME> command do?

The ip igmp querier query-max-response-time <RESPONSE-TIME> command configures the IGMP querier max response time value in seconds on an interface, depending on the command context you are in. The no form of this command sets the querier max response time value to the default of 10 seconds on an interface. Syntax: no ip igmp querier query-max-response-time <RESPONSE-TIME>.
For 6200 AOS-CX 10.06, what is the syntax of the ip igmp querier query-max-response-time <RESPONSE-TIME> command?
Syntax: no ip igmp querier query-max-response-time <RESPONSE-TIME>
For 6200 AOS-CX 10.06, what does the ip igmp robustness <VALUE> command do?

The ip igmp robustness <VALUE> command configures IGMP robustness on an interface, depending on the command context. The robustness parameter allows tuning for the expected packet loss on a subnet. The no form of this command sets the robustness value to the default of 2 on an interface. Syntax: no ip igmp robustness <VALUE>.
For 6200 AOS-CX 10.06, what is the syntax of the ip igmp robustness <VALUE> command?
Syntax: no ip igmp robustness <VALUE>
For 6200 AOS-CX 10.06, what does the ip igmp snooping apply access-list <ACL-NAME> command do?

The ip igmp snooping apply access-list <ACL-NAME> command configures the ACL on a particular interface to filter the IGMP join or leave packets based on rules set in the particular ACL name. The no form of this command unconfigures the rules set for the ACL. 398 AOS-CX 10.06 Command-Line Interface Guide NOTE: This configuration will override the ACL associated with IGMP snooping on the corresponding L2 VLAN. Syntax: no ip igmp snooping apply access-list <ACL-NAME>.

syntax: no ip igmp snooping apply access-list <ACL-NAME>

messages.content: For 6200 AOS-CX 10.06, what is the syntax of the ip igmp snooping apply access-list <ACL-NAME> command?
Syntax: no ip igmp snooping apply access-list <ACL-NAME>

syntax: no ip igmp snooping apply access-list <ACL-NAME>

messages.content: For 6200 AOS-CX 10.06, what does the ip igmp snooping auto <PORT-LIST> command do?

The ip igmp snooping auto <PORT-LIST> command configures the specified ports in auto mode. In auto mode traffic flow is controlled by the IGMP joins/leaves. Auto mode is the default. The no form of this command removes auto mode ports for the VLAN. Chapter 34 IGMP Snooping commands 399. Syntax: no ip igmp snooping auto <PORT-LIST>.
For 6200 AOS-CX 10.06, what is the syntax of the ip igmp snooping auto <PORT-LIST> command?
Syntax: no ip igmp snooping auto <PORT-LIST>
For 6200 AOS-CX 10.06, what does the ip igmp snooping fastlearn <PORT-LIST> command do?

The ip igmp snooping fastlearn <PORT-LIST> command enables the port to learn group information when receiving a topology change notification. By default, fast learn is not enabled on ports. The no form of this command disables fast learn on the specified ports. Syntax: no ip igmp snooping fastlearn <PORT-LIST>.
For 6200 AOS-CX 10.06, what is the syntax of the ip igmp snooping fastlearn <PORT-LIST> command?
Syntax: no ip igmp snooping fastlearn <PORT-LIST>
For 6200 AOS-CX 10.06, what does the ip igmp snooping fastleave <PORT-LIST> command do?

The ip igmp snooping fastleave <PORT-LIST> command enables the switch to immediately remove the IGMP client from its IGMP table and cease transmitting multicast traffic to the client. The no form of this command disables fastleave on the specified ports. Syntax: no ip igmp snooping fastleave <PORT-LIST>.
For 6200 AOS-CX 10.06, what is the syntax of the ip igmp snooping fastleave <PORT-LIST> command?
Syntax: no ip igmp snooping fastleave <PORT-LIST>
For 6200 AOS-CX 10.06, what does the ip igmp snooping forced-fastleave <PORT-LIST> command do?

The ip igmp snooping forced-fastleave <PORT-LIST> command configures the specified ports in forced fastleave mode. The no form of this command disables forced fastleave on the specified ports. Syntax: no ip igmp snooping forced-fastleave <PORT-LIST>.
For 6200 AOS-CX 10.06, what is the syntax of the ip igmp snooping forced-fastleave <PORT-LIST> command?
Syntax: no ip igmp snooping forced-fastleave <PORT-LIST>
For 6200 AOS-CX 10.06, what does the ip igmp snooping forward <PORT-LIST> command do?

The ip igmp snooping forward <PORT-LIST> command configures the specified ports in forward mode. In forward mode, traffic is always forwarded on this port, irrespective of joins. The no form of this command disables forward ports. Syntax: no ip igmp snooping forward <PORT-LIST>.
For 6200 AOS-CX 10.06, what is the syntax of the ip igmp snooping forward <PORT-LIST> command?
Syntax: no ip igmp snooping forward <PORT-LIST>
For 6200 AOS-CX 10.06, what does the interface loopback <ID> command do?

The interface loopback <ID> command creates a loopback interface and changes to the config-loopback-if context. Loopback interfaces are layer 3. The no form of this command deletes a loopback interface. Syntax: no interface loopback <ID>.
For 6200 AOS-CX 10.06, what is the syntax of the interface loopback <ID> command?
Syntax: no interface loopback <ID>
For 6200 AOS-CX 10.06, what does the interface vlan <VLAN-ID> command do?

The interface vlan <VLAN-ID> command creates an interface VLAN also know as an SVI (switched virtual interface) and changes to the config-if- vlan context. The specified VLAN must already be defined on the switch. The no form of this command deletes an interface VLAN. Syntax: no interface vlan <VLAN-ID>.
For 6200 AOS-CX 10.06, what is the syntax of the interface vlan <VLAN-ID> command?
Syntax: no interface vlan <VLAN-ID>
For 6200 AOS-CX 10.06, what does the interface tunnel <EXISTING-TUNNEL-NUMBER> command do?

The interface tunnel <EXISTING-TUNNEL-NUMBER> command creates or updates an IP tunnel. After you enter the command, the firmware switches to the configuration context for the tunnel. If the specified tunnel exists, this command switches to the context for the tunnel. By default, all tunnels are automatically assigned to the default VRF when they are created. The no form of this command deletes an existing IP tunnel. Syntax: no interface tunnel <EXISTING-TUNNEL-NUMBER>.
For 6200 AOS-CX 10.06, what is the syntax of the interface tunnel <EXISTING-TUNNEL-NUMBER> command?
Syntax: no interface tunnel <EXISTING-TUNNEL-NUMBER>
For 6200 AOS-CX 10.06, what does the ip address <IPv4-ADDR>/<MASK> command do?

The ip address <IPv4-ADDR>/<MASK> command sets the local IP address of a GRE tunnel. This address identifies the tunnel interface for routing. It must be on the same subnet as the tunnel address assigned on the remote device. The no form of this command deletes the local IP address assigned to a GRE tunnel. Syntax: no ip address <IPv4-ADDR>/<MASK>.
For 6200 AOS-CX 10.06, what is the syntax of the ip address <IPv4-ADDR>/<MASK> command?
Syntax: no ip address <IPv4-ADDR>/<MASK>
For 6200 AOS-CX 10.06, what does the IPv6 source-binding <VLAN-ID> <IPv6-ADDR> <MAC-ADDR> <IFNAME> command do?

The IPv6 source-binding <VLAN-ID> <IPv6-ADDR> <MAC-ADDR> <IFNAME> command adds static IPv6 client source binding information to the switch IPv6 binding database. Although DHCPv6 snooping is often used to dynamically populate the binding database, this command is available for manually adding entries to the switch IPv6 binding database. NOTE: Statically configured IPv6 binding information supersedes any dynamically collected binding information for the same client. The no form of this command removes the specified binding that was statically configured with the IPv6 source-binding command. The no form has no effect on bindings that were dynamically configured with DHCPv6 snooping. Syntax: no IPv6 source-binding <VLAN-ID> <IPv6-ADDR> <MAC-ADDR> <IFNAME>.
For 6200 AOS-CX 10.06, what is the syntax of the IPv6 source-binding <VLAN-ID> <IPv6-ADDR> <MAC-ADDR> <IFNAME> command?
Syntax: no IPv6 source-binding <VLAN-ID> <IPv6-ADDR> <MAC-ADDR> <IFNAME>
For 6200 AOS-CX 10.06, what does the IPv6 source-lockdown command do?

The IPv6 source-lockdown command enables IPv6 source lockdown for all VLANs on the selected interface (port). The no form of this command disables IPv6 source lockdown for the selected interface (port). Retries the IPv6 source lockdown hardware programming for a client identified by VLAN and IPv6 address. Shows all IPv6 static source binding information irrespective of source lockdown configuration. Shows summary or detailed IPv6 source lockdown information. When entered without parameters, summary status information for all interfaces (ports) in the binding database is shown. Syntax: IPv6 source-lockdown hardware retry <VLAN-ID> <IPv6-ADDR>.
For 6200 AOS-CX 10.06, what is the syntax of the IPv6 source-lockdown command?
Syntax: IPv6 source-lockdown hardware retry <VLAN-ID> <IPv6-ADDR>
For 6200 AOS-CX 10.06, what does the IPv6 address <global-unicast-address> command do?

The IPv6 address <global-unicast-address> command sets a global unicast address on the interface. The no form of this command removes the global unicast address on the interface. NOTE: This command automatically creates an IPv6 link-local address on the interface. However, it does not add the IPv6 address link-local command to the running configuration. If you remove the IPv6 address, the link-local address is also removed. To maintain the link-local address, you must manually execute the IPv6 address link-local command. Syntax: no IPv6 address <global-unicast-address>.
For 6200 AOS-CX 10.06, what is the syntax of the IPv6 address <global-unicast-address> command?
Syntax: no IPv6 address <global-unicast-address>
For 6200 AOS-CX 10.06, what does the ssh password-authentication command do?

The ssh password-authentication command enables the password-based authentication method for use with SSH clients. The no form of this command disables the password-based authentication method for use with SSH clients. Syntax: no ssh password-authentication.
For 6200 AOS-CX 10.06, what is the syntax of the ssh password-authentication command?
Syntax: no ssh password-authentication
For 6200 AOS-CX 10.06, what does the interface loopback <INSTANCE> command do?

The interface loopback <INSTANCE> command creates a loopback interface and enters loopback configuration mode. The no form of this command deletes a loopback interface. Syntax: no interface loopback <INSTANCE>.
For 6200 AOS-CX 10.06, what is the syntax of the interface loopback <INSTANCE> command?
Syntax: no interface loopback <INSTANCE>
For 6200 AOS-CX 10.06, what does the no ntp server <IP-ADDR> command do?

The no ntp server <IP-ADDR> command defines an NTP server to use for time synchronization, or updates the settings of an existing server with new values. Up to eight servers can be defined. The no form of this command removes a configured NTP server. Syntax: no ntp server <IP-ADDR> [burst] [iburst] [prefer] [key-id <KEY-NUM>].
For 6200 AOS-CX 10.06, what is the syntax of the no ntp server <IP-ADDR> command?
Syntax: no ntp server <IP-ADDR> [burst] [iburst] [prefer] [key-id <KEY-NUM>]
For 6200 AOS-CX 10.06, what does the ip ospf <PROCESS-ID> area <AREA-ID> command do?

The ip ospf <PROCESS-ID> area <AREA-ID> command runs the OSPF protocol on the interface with the configured IPv4 address for the area specified. The interfaces which have an IP address configured in this network or in a subset of this network, will participate in the OSPF protocol. To move an interface to a new area, unmap the existing area and then associate a new area with the interface. The no form of this command disables OSPF on the interface and removes the interface from the area. Interfaces which have an IP address configured on the network or in a subset of the network, stop participating in the OSPF protocol. Sets the authentication type that will be used for authentication with the neighbor router. Syntax: ip ospf authentication {message-digest | simple-text | null | keychain}.

syntax: ip ospf authentication {message-digest | simple-text | null | keychain}

messages.content: For 6200 AOS-CX 10.06, what is the syntax of the ip ospf <PROCESS-ID> area <AREA-ID> command?
Syntax: ip ospf authentication {message-digest | simple-text | null | keychain}

syntax: ip ospf authentication {message-digest | simple-text | null | keychain}

messages.content: For 6200 AOS-CX 10.06, what does the IPv6 ospfv3 <PROCESS-ID> area <AREA-ID> command do?

The IPv6 ospfv3 <PROCESS-ID> area <AREA-ID> command runs the OSPFv3 protocol on the interface for the area specified. To move an interface to a new area, unmap the existing area and then associate a new area with the interface. The no form of this command disables OSPF on the interface and removes the interface from the area. Interfaces which have an IP address configured on the network or in a subset of the network, stop participating in the OSPF protocol Configures null authentication on an interface which disables IPsec authentication. Configures IPSec AH authentication. OSPFv3 interfaces that have IPsec configured at the interface context will not use area level IPsec. The no form of this command removes IPsec AH authentication for the specified area. Sets the cost (metric) associated with a particular interface. Syntax: IPv6 ospfv3 authentication null.
For 6200 AOS-CX 10.06, what is the syntax of the IPv6 ospfv3 <PROCESS-ID> area <AREA-ID> command?
Syntax: IPv6 ospfv3 authentication null
For 6200 AOS-CX 10.06, what does the crypto pki certificate <CERT-NAME> command do?

The crypto pki certificate <CERT-NAME> command creates a leaf certificate and changes to its context config-cert-<CERT-NAME>. If the specified leaf certificate exists, this command changes to its context. The first time the switch starts it creates a self-signed, default leaf certificate called local-cert. This certificate is used by any switch application that does not have an associated leaf certificate. The no form of this command deletes the specified leaf certificate. The default leaf certificate local-cert cannot be deleted. Syntax: no crypto pki certificate <CERT-NAME>.
For 6200 AOS-CX 10.06, what is the syntax of the crypto pki certificate <CERT-NAME> command?
Syntax: no crypto pki certificate <CERT-NAME>

section: aaa authentication port-access dot1x authenticator auth-method eap-radius

messages.content: For 6200 AOS-CX 10.06, what does the aaa authentication port-access dot1x authenticator auth-method eap-radius command do?

The aaa authentication port-access dot1x authenticator auth-method eap-radius command configures the authentication mechanism used to control access to the network. The configured authentication method will be used to authenticate 802.1X clients. The no form of the command resets the authentication mechanism to the default, eap-radius. Syntax: no aaa authentication port-access dot1x authenticator auth-method eap-radius.

command: aaa authentication port-access dot1x authenticator auth-method eap-radius
syntax: no aaa authentication port-access dot1x authenticator auth-method eap-radius

section: aaa authentication port-access dot1x authenticator auth-method eap-radius

messages.content: For 6200 AOS-CX 10.06, what is the syntax of the aaa authentication port-access dot1x authenticator auth-method eap-radius command?
Syntax: no aaa authentication port-access dot1x authenticator auth-method eap-radius

command: aaa authentication port-access dot1x authenticator auth-method eap-radius
syntax: no aaa authentication port-access dot1x authenticator auth-method eap-radius

section: aaa authentication port-access dot1x authenticator cached-reauth

messages.content: For 6200 AOS-CX 10.06, what does the aaa authentication port-access dot1x authenticator cached-reauth command do?

The aaa authentication port-access dot1x authenticator cached-reauth command enables cached reauthentication on a port. Cached reauthentication allows 802.1X reauthentications to succeed when the RADIUS server is unavailable. Users already authenticated retain their currently assigned RADIUS attributes. The no form of the command disables the cached reauthentication on a port. Configures the period during which an authenticated client, which has failed to reauthenticate because the RADIUS server is unreachable, remains authenticated. The no form of the command resets the cached reauthentication period to the default, 30 seconds. Configures the period the port waits to retransmit the next EAPOL request identity frame on an 802.1X enabled port that has no authenticated clients. The no form of the command resets the discovery period to the default, 30 seconds. Syntax: aaa authentication port-access dot1x authenticator cached-reauth-period <PERIOD>.

command: aaa authentication port-access dot1x authenticator cached-reauth
syntax: aaa authentication port-access dot1x authenticator cached-reauth-period <PERIOD>

section: aaa authentication port-access dot1x authenticator cached-reauth

messages.content: For 6200 AOS-CX 10.06, what is the syntax of the aaa authentication port-access dot1x authenticator cached-reauth command?
Syntax: aaa authentication port-access dot1x authenticator cached-reauth-period <PERIOD>

command: aaa authentication port-access dot1x authenticator cached-reauth
syntax: aaa authentication port-access dot1x authenticator cached-reauth-period <PERIOD>

section: aaa authentication port-access dot1x authenticator reauth

messages.content: For 6200 AOS-CX 10.06, what does the aaa authentication port-access dot1x authenticator reauth command do?

The aaa authentication port-access dot1x authenticator reauth command enables periodic reauthentication of authenticated clients on the port. The no form of the command disables periodic reauthentication. Configures the period after which the authenticated clients are reauthenticated on the port. You must enable reauthentication on the port before configuring the reauthentication period. The no form of the command resets the reauthentication period to the default, 3600 seconds. Clears the 802.1X authentication statistics associated with the port and all the authenticator clients attached to this port. If no interface is specified, the statistics is cleared for all 802.1X enabled ports. Shows information about active 802.1X authentication sessions. The output can be filtered by interface or MAC address. Shows information about 802.1X ports. Syntax: aaa authentication port-access dot1x authenticator reauth-period <PERIOD>.

command: aaa authentication port-access dot1x authenticator reauth
syntax: aaa authentication port-access dot1x authenticator reauth-period <PERIOD>

section: aaa authentication port-access dot1x authenticator reauth

messages.content: For 6200 AOS-CX 10.06, what is the syntax of the aaa authentication port-access dot1x authenticator reauth command?
Syntax: aaa authentication port-access dot1x authenticator reauth-period <PERIOD>

command: aaa authentication port-access dot1x authenticator reauth
syntax: aaa authentication port-access dot1x authenticator reauth-period <PERIOD>

messages.content: For 6200 AOS-CX 10.06, what does the aaa authentication port-access mac-auth cached-reauth command do?

The aaa authentication port-access mac-auth cached-reauth command enables cached reauthentication on a port. Cached reauthentication allows MAC reauthentications to succeed when the RADIUS server is unavailable. Users who are already authenticated, retain their currently assigned RADIUS attributes. The no form of the command disables cached reauthentication. Configures the period during which an authenticated client, which has failed to reauthenticate because the RADIUS server is unreachable, remains authenticated. The no form of the command resets the cached reauthentication period to the default, 30 seconds. Enables and configures the global password that the switch must use for MAC authentication. The password can be either in ciphertext or plaintext format. The no form of the command disables the password for MAC authentication. Syntax: aaa authentication port-access mac-auth cached-reauth-period <PERIOD>.
For 6200 AOS-CX 10.06, what is the syntax of the aaa authentication port-access mac-auth cached-reauth command?
Syntax: aaa authentication port-access mac-auth cached-reauth-period <PERIOD>
For 6200 AOS-CX 10.06, what does the aaa authentication port-access mac-auth reauth command do?

The aaa authentication port-access mac-auth reauth command enables periodic MAC reauthentication of authenticated clients on the port. The no form of the command disables periodic MAC reauthentication on the port. Configures the period after which MAC authenticated clients must be reauthenticated on the port. You must first enable MAC reauthentication on the port before configuring the MAC reauthentication period. The no form of the command resets the MAC reauthentication period to the default, 3600 seconds. Clears the MAC authentication statistics associated with the port and all the authenticator state machines associated to this port. If no interface is specified, the statistics is cleared for all MAC authentication enabled ports. Shows information about MAC authentication clients status. The output can be filtered by interface or MAC address. Syntax: aaa authentication port-access mac-auth reauth-period <PERIOD>.
For 6200 AOS-CX 10.06, what is the syntax of the aaa authentication port-access mac-auth reauth command?
Syntax: aaa authentication port-access mac-auth reauth-period <PERIOD>
For 6200 AOS-CX 10.06, what does the qos queue-profile <NAME> command do?

The qos queue-profile <NAME> command creates a new QoS queue profile and switches to the config-queue context for the profile. Or, if the specified QoS queue profile exists, this command switches to the config-queue context for the profile. A queue profile maps queues to local-priority values. Each profile supports up to eight queues, starting at 0 and incrementing sequentially to the number of desired queues. A queue profile named factory-default is defined by default and is automatically applied to all interfaces. It cannot be edited or deleted. Use the command show qos queue-profile factory- default to view this profile. Do not use show running-configuration, as it will only display changes from the initial values. The no form of this command removes the specified QoS queue profile. Syntax: no qos queue-profile <NAME>.
For 6200 AOS-CX 10.06, what is the syntax of the qos queue-profile <NAME> command?
Syntax: no qos queue-profile <NAME>
For 6200 AOS-CX 10.06, what does the no aaa accounting port-access command do?

The no aaa accounting port-access command configures port access accounting information that is captured for 802.1X and MAC-authenticated clients. Defines port access accounting as being local (with the parameter local) (the default). Or defines port access accounting as being remote (with the parameter group <GROUP-NAME>) with a sequence of remote RADIUS servers in a single RADIUS server group to be accessed for port access accounting purposes. For remote RADIUS port access accounting, the information is sent to the first reachable remote RADIUS server in the specified group. If a user-defined RADIUS server group is named in your command, it must exist. The no form of this command works as follows: • no aaa accounting port-access: Globally unconfigures port access accounting. Syntax:
- no aaa accounting port-access local
- no aaa accounting port-access group
- no aaa accounting port-access interim.

syntax:
- no aaa accounting port-access local
- no aaa accounting port-access group
- no aaa accounting port-access interim

messages.content: For 6200 AOS-CX 10.06, what is the syntax of the no aaa accounting port-access command?
Syntax: - no aaa accounting port-access local - no aaa accounting port-access group - no aaa accounting port-access interim

syntax:
- no aaa accounting port-access local
- no aaa accounting port-access group
- no aaa accounting port-access interim

messages.content: For 6200 AOS-CX 10.06, what does the ip rip all-ip enable command do?

The ip rip all-ip enable command enables RIP process for all RIP enabled IP addresses configured on interface. The no form of this command disables RIP process on the interface. Syntax: no ip rip all-ip enable.
For 6200 AOS-CX 10.06, what is the syntax of the ip rip all-ip enable command?
Syntax: no ip rip all-ip enable
For 6200 AOS-CX 10.06, what does the ip rip all-ip disable command do?

The ip rip all-ip disable command disables RIP process for all RIP enabled IP addresses configured on the interface. The no form of this command enables RIP process for all RIP enabled IP addresses configured on the interface. Syntax: no ip rip all-ip disable.
For 6200 AOS-CX 10.06, what is the syntax of the ip rip all-ip disable command?
Syntax: no ip rip all-ip disable
For 6200 AOS-CX 10.06, what does the ip rip all-ip send disable command do?

The ip rip all-ip send disable command disables interface from sending RIP packets for all RIP enabled IP addresses. The no form of this command enables interface to send RIP packets for all RIP enabled IP addresses. Syntax: no ip rip all-ip send disable.

syntax: no ip rip all-ip send disable

messages.content: For 6200 AOS-CX 10.06, what is the syntax of the ip rip all-ip send disable command?
Syntax: no ip rip all-ip send disable

syntax: no ip rip all-ip send disable

messages.content: For 6200 AOS-CX 10.06, what does the router ripng <PROCESS-ID> [vrf <VRF-NAME>] command do?

The router ripng <PROCESS-ID> [vrf <VRF-NAME>] command creates RIPng process if not already created and enters the router ripng <PROCESS-ID> context for the VRF mentioned. If no VRF is mentioned, a default is used. Only one RIPng process is allowed per VRF. The no form of this command deletes the RIPng instance for the VRF. If no VRF is mentioned the default is deleted. Syntax: no router ripng <PROCESS-ID> [vrf <VRF-NAME>].
For 6200 AOS-CX 10.06, what is the syntax of the router ripng <PROCESS-ID> [vrf <VRF-NAME>] command?
Syntax: no router ripng <PROCESS-ID> [vrf <VRF-NAME>]
For 6200 AOS-CX 10.06, what does the vrf command do?
The vrf command is documented in this guide. Syntax: VRF name for VRF.
For 6200 AOS-CX 10.06, what is the syntax of the vrf command?
Syntax: VRF name for VRF
For 6200 AOS-CX 10.06, what does the show ubt information command do?
The show ubt information command shows SAC and UAC information for UBT. Specifying a zone name displays UBT information for that zone. Syntax: show ubt information zone <ZONE-NAME>.
For 6200 AOS-CX 10.06, what is the syntax of the show ubt information command?
Syntax: show ubt information zone <ZONE-NAME>

The show ubt state command is documented in this guide. Syntax:
- show ubt state zone <ZONE-NAME>
- show ubt state zone <ZONE-NAME> uac-ip <UAC-ADDR>.

syntax:
- show ubt state zone <ZONE-NAME>
- show ubt state zone <ZONE-NAME> uac-ip <UAC-ADDR>

Syntax: - show ubt state zone <ZONE-NAME> - show ubt state zone <ZONE-NAME> uac-ip <UAC-ADDR>

syntax:
- show ubt state zone <ZONE-NAME>

messages.content: For 6200 AOS-CX 10.06, what does the show ubt state zone <ZONE-NAME> command do?

The show ubt state zone <ZONE-NAME> command shows the global UBT state. Specifying a zone shows the UBT state of that zone. Specifying a UAC IP address shows the UBT state of that UAC. Syntax: show ubt state zone <ZONE-NAME> uac-ip <UAC-ADDR>.

Syntax: show ubt state zone <ZONE-NAME> uac-ip <UAC-ADDR>

The show ubt statistics command is documented in this guide. Syntax:
- show ubt statistics zone <ZONE-NAME>
- show ubt statistics zone <ZONE-NAME> uac-ip <UAC-ADDR>.

syntax:
- show ubt statistics zone <ZONE-NAME>
- show ubt statistics zone <ZONE-NAME> uac-ip <UAC-ADDR>

Syntax: - show ubt statistics zone <ZONE-NAME> - show ubt statistics zone <ZONE-NAME> uac-ip <UAC-ADDR>

syntax:
- show ubt statistics zone <ZONE-NAME>
- show ubt statistics zone <ZONE-NAME> uac-ip <UAC-ADDR>

messages.content: For 6200 AOS-CX 10.06, what does the vlan <VLAN-LIST> command do?

The vlan <VLAN-LIST> command creates a VLAN and changes to the config-vlan-id context for the VLAN. By default, the VLAN is enabled. To disable a VLAN, use the no shutdown command. If the specified VLAN exists, this command changes to the config-vlan-id context for the VLAN. If a range of VLANs is specified, the context does not change. The no form of this command removes a VLAN. VLAN 1 is the default VLAN and cannot be deleted. Creates an access interface and assigns an VLAN ID to it. Only one VLAN ID can be assigned to each access interface. VLANs can only be assigned to a non-routed (layer 2) interface or LAG interface. By default, all interfaces are routed (layer 3) when created. Use the no routing command to disable routing on an interface and change the interface to a layer 2 interface. Syntax: vlan access <VLAN-ID>.
For 6200 AOS-CX 10.06, what is the syntax of the vlan <VLAN-LIST> command?
Syntax: vlan access <VLAN-ID>
For 6200 AOS-CX 10.06, what does the show spanning-tree mst-config command do?
The show spanning-tree mst-config command shows MSTP instance and corresponding VLAN information. Syntax: show spanning-tree mst-config [vsx-peer].
For 6200 AOS-CX 10.06, what is the syntax of the show spanning-tree mst-config command?
Syntax: show spanning-tree mst-config [vsx-peer]
For 6200 AOS-CX 10.06, what does the show spanning-tree mst detail command do?
The show spanning-tree mst detail command shows detailed information for all MST instances. Syntax: show spanning-tree mst detail [vsx-peer].
For 6200 AOS-CX 10.06, what is the syntax of the show spanning-tree mst detail command?
Syntax: show spanning-tree mst detail [vsx-peer]
For 6200 AOS-CX 10.06, what does the show spanning-tree mst <INSTANCE-ID> command do?
The show spanning-tree mst <INSTANCE-ID> command displays MSTP configurations for the given instance ID. Syntax: show spanning-tree mst <INSTANCE-ID> [vsx-peer].
For 6200 AOS-CX 10.06, what is the syntax of the show spanning-tree mst <INSTANCE-ID> command?
Syntax: show spanning-tree mst <INSTANCE-ID> [vsx-peer]
For 6200 AOS-CX 10.06, what does the show spanning-tree mst <INSTANCE-ID> detail command do?

The show spanning-tree mst <INSTANCE-ID> detail command displays MSTP configurations for the given instance ID with corresponding port details. Syntax: show spanning-tree mst <INSTANCE-ID> detail [vsx-peer].
For 6200 AOS-CX 10.06, what is the syntax of the show spanning-tree mst <INSTANCE-ID> detail command?
Syntax: show spanning-tree mst <INSTANCE-ID> detail [vsx-peer]
For 6200 AOS-CX 10.06, how do you configure CoPP?

To configure CoPP, follow the documented procedure: 1. Configure the default CoPP policy, edit an existing policy, or create a policy with the command copp- 2. Add, edit, or remove classes in the policy with the command class 3. If the policy is not the active policy on the switch, apply it with the command apply copp-policy 4. Review the CoPP policy configuration settings with the command show copp-policy. Commands shown in the procedure include: policy..
For 6200 AOS-CX 10.06, what does the apply copp-policy command do?

The apply copp-policy command applies a CoPP policy to the switch, replacing the policy that is in effect. There may be a brief interruption in traffic flow to the management processor while the switch implements the change. Enter the no apply copp-policy <NAME> command with the name of a CoPP policy to unapply a CoPP policy and apply the default CoPP policy. This will only take effect if the specified policy is actively applied. Since there must always be a CoPP policy applied, this command effectively attempts to replace the applied CoPP policy with the default CoPP policy. The default CoPP policy cannot be unapplied using this command. Syntax: apply copp-policy { <NAME> | default }.
For 6200 AOS-CX 10.06, what is the syntax of the apply copp-policy command?
Syntax: apply copp-policy { <NAME> | default }
For 6200 AOS-CX 10.06, what is the syntax of the class command?
Syntax: class <CLASS> {drop | priority <PRIORITY> rate <RATE> [burst <BURST>]}
For 6200 AOS-CX 10.06, what does the clear copp-policy statistics command do?
The clear copp-policy statistics command resets statistics for all CoPP classes to zero. Syntax: clear copp-policy statistics.
For 6200 AOS-CX 10.06, what is the syntax of the clear copp-policy statistics command?
Syntax: clear copp-policy statistics
For 6200 AOS-CX 10.06, what does the default-class priority <PRIORITY> rate <RATE> [burst <BURST>] command do?

The default-class priority <PRIORITY> rate <RATE> [burst <BURST>] command configures the default class that is automatically defined for all CoPP policies. The default class cannot be removed, but its configuration can be changed. The default class is applied to traffic that does not match any other class defined for a policy. Syntax: default-class priority <PRIORITY> rate <RATE> [burst <BURST>].
For 6200 AOS-CX 10.06, what is the syntax of the default-class priority <PRIORITY> rate <RATE> [burst <BURST>] command?
Syntax: default-class priority <PRIORITY> rate <RATE> [burst <BURST>]
For 6200 AOS-CX 10.06, what does the reset copp-policy { <NAME> | default } command do?

The reset copp-policy { <NAME> | default } command resets an active CoPP policy to match the settings that are currently in effect for the active policy on the switch. Changes made to the active policy that could not be activated are removed from the active policy. When the switch fails to add or modify a class in an active CoPP policy, it is possible the active policy settings on the switch may be out of sync with those defined in the policy. Syntax: reset copp-policy { <NAME> | default }.

For 6200 AOS-CX 10.06, what does the guide say about Management of rotated log files?

Management of rotated log files: Rotated log files are compressed and stored locally in /var/log/, regardless of the remote host configuration. Rotated log files are stored with respective time extension to the granularity of hour in the format file1-YYYYMMDDHH.gz (for example, messages-2015080715.gz). Rotated log files are replaced when the number of old rotated log files exceeds three. The newly rotated log file replaces the oldest rotated log file. Remote transfer of rotated log files Only the TFTP protocol is supported for remote transfer, and both IPv4 and IPv6 addresses are supported. Only newly rotated log files are transferred to the remote host during the log rotation. Previously rotated log files are not transferred. After a file is successfully transferred, it is removed from the switch local path.
For 6200 AOS-CX 10.06, what does the logrotate maxsize <MAX-SIZE> command do?

The logrotate maxsize <MAX-SIZE> command specifies the maximum allowed log file size. The no form of this command resets the size of the log file to the default (100 MB). Sets the rotate period for the event logs, stored in the /var/log/event.log file, and authentication logs, stored in the /var/log/auth.log file. Defaults to daily. A log file that exceeds either the logrotate <MAX-SIZE> value or the logrotate period (whichever happens first), triggers rotation for that log file. The no form of this command resets the log rotation period to the default. Syntax: logrotate maxsize <MAX-SIZE>.
For 6200 AOS-CX 10.06, what is the syntax of the logrotate maxsize <MAX-SIZE> command?
Syntax: logrotate maxsize <MAX-SIZE>

For 6200 AOS-CX 10.06, how do you troubleshoot?

To troubleshoot, follow the documented procedure: 1. Modify the ACL to allow the ping traffic 2. Unapply the ACL from egress (8400/8320/8325 switches) or Control Plane 3. Ping a destination which is not matched by the ACL. For example, if the ACL is blocking traffic based on. Commands shown in the procedure include: PING 100.1.2.10 (100.1.2.10) 100(128) bytes of data; ping: sendmsg: Operation not permitted; ping: sendmsg: Operation not permitted; ping: sendmsg: Operation not permitted; ping: sendmsg: Operation not permitted; ping: sendmsg: Operation not permitted.
