Show tech command list for the ISP feature Task Displaying versions of all present programmable devices. Displaying stored log files from any ISP updates on the system.
For 6200 AOS-CX 10.06, what does the clear update-log command do?
The clear update-log command clears stored log files of any In-System Programming updates on the system. Syntax: clear update-log.
For 6200 AOS-CX 10.06, what is the syntax of the clear update-log command?
Syntax: clear update-log
For 6200 AOS-CX 10.06, what is the syntax of the show needed-updates command?
Syntax: show needed-updates [next-boot [primary|secondary]]
For 6200 AOS-CX 10.06, what does the show selftest command do?
The show selftest command is documented in this guide. Syntax: show selftest [brief] [vsx-peer].
For 6200 AOS-CX 10.06, what is the syntax of the show selftest command?
Syntax: show selftest [brief] [vsx-peer]
For 6200 AOS-CX 10.06, what does the erase all zeroize command do?

The erase all zeroize command restores the switch to its factory default configuration. You will be prompted before the procedure starts. Once complete, the switch will restart from the primary image with factory default settings. NOTE: Back up all data before running this command as all configuration settings will be lost. Syntax: erase all zeroize.
For 6200 AOS-CX 10.06, what is the syntax of the erase all zeroize command?
Syntax: erase all zeroize
For 6200 AOS-CX 10.06, what is the syntax of the terminal-monitor {notify <event|debug|all> | severity <level> | filter keyword} command?
Syntax: terminal-monitor {notify <event|debug|all> | severity <level> | filter keyword}
For 6200 AOS-CX 10.06, what does the show terminal-monitor command do?
The show terminal-monitor command shows whether the terminal monitoring is enabled or disabled. Syntax: show terminal-monitor.
For 6200 AOS-CX 10.06, what is the syntax of the show terminal-monitor command?
Syntax: show terminal-monitor

section: Troubleshooting Web UI and REST API access issues

messages.content: For 6200 AOS-CX 10.06, how do you troubleshoot Web UI and REST API access issues?

To troubleshoot Web UI and REST API access issues, follow the documented procedure: 1. Log out from one of the existing sessions 2. If the session cookie is lost and it is not possible to log out of the session, then wait for the session idle.

task: Troubleshooting Web UI and REST API access issues

messages.content: For 6200 AOS-CX 10.06, what does the guide say about About AOS-CX?

About AOS-CX: AOS-CX introduction AOS-CX is a new, modern, fully programmable operating system built using a database-centric design that ensures higher availability and dynamic software process changes for reduced downtime. In addition to robust hardware reliability, the AOS-CX operating system includes additional software elements not available with traditional systems, including: • Automated visibility to help IT organizations scale: The Aruba Network Analytics Engine allows IT to monitor and troubleshoot network, system, application, and security-related issues easily through simple scripts. This engine comes with a built-in time series database that enables customers and developers to create software modules that allow historical troubleshooting, as well as analysis of historical trends to predict and avoid future problems due to scale, security, and performance bottlenecks.
For 6200 AOS-CX 10.06, what does the guide say about Initial configuration?

Initial configuration: Perform the initial configuration of a factory default switch using one of the following methods: • Load a switch configuration using zero-touch provisioning (ZTP). When ZTP is used, the configuration is loaded from a server automatically when the switch booted from the factory default configuration. • Connect to the switch wirelessly with a mobile device through Bluetooth, and use the Aruba CX Mobile App to deploy an initial configuration from a provided template. The template you choose during the deployment process determines how the management interface is configured. Optionally, as the final deployment step, you can select to import the switch into Net Edit through a WiFI connection to the Net Edit server. Alternatively, you can use the Aruba CX Mobile App to manually configure switch settings and features for a subset of the features you can configure using the CLI. You can also access the CLI through the mobile application.
For 6200 AOS-CX 10.06, how do you troubleshoot Bluetooth connections?

To troubleshoot Bluetooth connections, follow the documented procedure: 1. Verify the model number and serial number of the switch to which you are attempting to connect 2. Use the Bluetooth settings on your mobile device to pair and connect the switch to your mobile device.
For 6200 AOS-CX 10.06, how do you configure the management interface?

To configure the management interface, follow the documented procedure: 1. Switch to the management interface context with the command interface mgmt 2. By default, the management interface on the management port is enabled. If it was disabled, reenable it 3. Use the command ip dhcp to configure the management interface to automatically obtain an address 4. SSH is enabled by default on the management VRF. If disabled, enable SSH with the command ssh.
For 6200 AOS-CX 10.06, what does the default-gateway <IP-ADDR> command do?

The default-gateway <IP-ADDR> command assigns an IPv4 or IPv6 default gateway to the management interface. An IPv4 default gateway can only be configured if a static IPv4 address was assigned to the management interface. An IPv6 default gateway can only be configured if a static IPv6 address was assigned to the management interface. The default gateway should be on the same network segment. The no form of this command removes the default gateway from the management interface. Syntax: default-gateway <IP-ADDR>.
For 6200 AOS-CX 10.06, what is the syntax of the default-gateway <IP-ADDR> command?
Syntax: default-gateway <IP-ADDR>
For 6200 AOS-CX 10.06, what does the ip static command do?

The ip static command assigns an IPv4 or IPv6 address to the management interface. The no form of this command removes the IP address from the management interface and sets the interface to operate as a DHCP client. Assigns a primary or secondary IPv4 or IPv6 DNS server to the management interface. IPv4 DNS servers can only be configured if a static IPv4 address was assigned to the management interface. IPv6 DNS servers can only be configured if a static IPv6 address was assigned to the management interface. The default gateway should be on the same network segment. 32 AOS-CX 10.06 Fundamentals Guide The no form of this command removes the DNS servers from the management interface. Syntax: ip static <IP-ADDR>/<MASK>.
For 6200 AOS-CX 10.06, what is the syntax of the ip static command?
Syntax: ip static <IP-ADDR>/<MASK>
For 6200 AOS-CX 10.06, what does the show interface mgmt command do?
The show interface mgmt command shows status and configuration information for the management interface. Syntax: show interface mgmt [vsx-peer].
For 6200 AOS-CX 10.06, what is the syntax of the show interface mgmt command?
Syntax: show interface mgmt [vsx-peer]
For 6200 AOS-CX 10.06, what does the ntp authentication command do?
The ntp authentication command enables support for authentication when communicating with an NTP server. The no form of this command disables authentication support. Syntax: ntp authentication.
For 6200 AOS-CX 10.06, what is the syntax of the ntp authentication command?
Syntax: ntp authentication
For 6200 AOS-CX 10.06, what does the ntp authentication-key command do?

The ntp authentication-key command defines an authentication key that is used to secure the exchange with an NTP time server. This command provides protection against accidentally synchronizing to a time source that is not trusted. The no form of this command removes an authentication key. Syntax: ntp authentication-key <KEY-ID> {md5 | sha1} <PASSWORD> [trusted].
For 6200 AOS-CX 10.06, what is the syntax of the ntp authentication-key command?
Syntax: ntp authentication-key <KEY-ID> {md5 | sha1} <PASSWORD> [trusted]
For 6200 AOS-CX 10.06, what does the ntp disable command do?
The ntp disable command disables the NTP client on the switch. The NTP client is disabled by default. Syntax: ntp disable.
For 6200 AOS-CX 10.06, what is the syntax of the ntp disable command?
Syntax: ntp disable
For 6200 AOS-CX 10.06, what does the ntp enable command do?

The ntp enable command enables the NTP client on the switch to automatically adjust the local time and date on the switch. The NTP client is disabled by default. The no form of this command disables the NTP client. Syntax: ntp enable.
For 6200 AOS-CX 10.06, what is the syntax of the ntp enable command?
Syntax: ntp enable
For 6200 AOS-CX 10.06, what does the ntp server command do?

The ntp server command defines an NTP server to use for time synchronization, or updates the settings of an existing server with new values. Up to eight servers can be defined. The no form of this command removes a configured NTP server. Syntax: ntp server <IP-ADDR> [key <KEY-NUM>] [minpoll <MIN-NUM>] [maxpoll <MAX-NUM>][burst | iburst] [prefer] [version <VER-NUM>].
For 6200 AOS-CX 10.06, what is the syntax of the ntp server command?
Syntax: ntp server <IP-ADDR> [key <KEY-NUM>] [minpoll <MIN-NUM>] [maxpoll <MAX-NUM>][burst | iburst] [prefer] [version <VER-NUM>]
For 6200 AOS-CX 10.06, what does the ntp trusted-key command do?

The ntp trusted-key command sets a key as trusted. When NTP authentication is enabled, the switch only synchronizes with time servers that transmit packets containing a trusted key. The no form of this command removes the trusted designation from a key. Syntax: ntp trusted-key <KEY-ID>.
For 6200 AOS-CX 10.06, what is the syntax of the ntp trusted-key command?
Syntax: ntp trusted-key <KEY-ID>
For 6200 AOS-CX 10.06, what does the ntp vrf command do?
The ntp vrf command specifies the VRF on which the NTP client communicates with an NTP server. Syntax: ntp vrf <VRF-NAME>.
For 6200 AOS-CX 10.06, what is the syntax of the ntp vrf command?
Syntax: ntp vrf <VRF-NAME>
For 6200 AOS-CX 10.06, what does the show ntp associations command do?

The show ntp associations command shows the status of the connection to each NTP server. The following information is displayed for each server: • Tally code: The first character is the Tally code: ◦ ◦ ◦ ◦ (blank): No state information available (e.g. non-responding server) x: Out of tolerance (discarded by intersection algorithm).: Discarded by table overflow (not used) -: Out of tolerance (discarded by the cluster algorithm) ◦ +: Good and a preferred remote peer or server (included by the combine algorithm) ◦ #: Good remote peer or server, but not utilized (ready as a backup source) ◦ *: Remote peer or server presently used as a primary reference ◦ o: PPS peer (when the prefer peer is valid) • ID: Server number. • NAME: NTP server FQDN/IP address (Only the first 24 characters of the name are displayed). Syntax: show ntp associations [vsx-peer].
For 6200 AOS-CX 10.06, what is the syntax of the show ntp associations command?
Syntax: show ntp associations [vsx-peer]
For 6200 AOS-CX 10.06, what does the show ntp authentication-keys command do?
The show ntp authentication-keys command shows the currently defined authentication keys. Syntax: show ntp authentication-keys [vsx-peer].
For 6200 AOS-CX 10.06, what is the syntax of the show ntp authentication-keys command?
Syntax: show ntp authentication-keys [vsx-peer]
For 6200 AOS-CX 10.06, what does the show ntp servers command do?
The show ntp servers command shows all configured NTP servers. Syntax: show ntp servers [vsx-peer].
For 6200 AOS-CX 10.06, what is the syntax of the show ntp servers command?
Syntax: show ntp servers [vsx-peer]
For 6200 AOS-CX 10.06, what does the show ntp statistics command do?

The show ntp statistics command shows global NTP statistics. The following information is displayed: • Rx-pkts: Total NTP packets received. • Current Version Rx-pkts: Number of NTP packets that match the current NTP version. • Old Version Rx-pkts: Number of NTP packets that match the previous NTP version. • Error pkts: Packets dropped due to all other error reasons. • Auth-failed pkts: Packets dropped due to authentication failure. • Declined pkts: Packets denied access for any reason. • Restricted pkts: Packets dropped due to NTP access control. • Rate-limited pkts: Number of packets discarded due to rate limitation. • KOD pkts: Number of Kiss of Death packets sent. Syntax: show ntp statistics [vsx-peer].
For 6200 AOS-CX 10.06, what is the syntax of the show ntp statistics command?
Syntax: show ntp statistics [vsx-peer]
For 6200 AOS-CX 10.06, what does the show ntp status command do?
The show ntp status command shows the status of NTP on the switch. Syntax: show ntp status [vsx-peer].
For 6200 AOS-CX 10.06, what is the syntax of the show ntp status command?
Syntax: show ntp status [vsx-peer]
For 6200 AOS-CX 10.06, how do you configure a layer 2 interface?

To configure a layer 2 interface, follow the documented procedure: 1. Change to the interface configuration context for the interface with the command interface 2. Set the interface MTU (maximum transmission unit) with the command mtu 3. Review interface configuration settings with the command show interface.
For 6200 AOS-CX 10.06, what does the default interface <INTERFACE-ID> command do?

The default interface <INTERFACE-ID> command sets an interface (or a range of interfaces) to factory default values. Associates descriptive information with an interface to help administrators and operators identify the purpose or role of an interface. The no form of this command removes a description from an interface. Enables auto-negotiation of Energy-Efficient Ethernet (EEE) on an interface. EEE Negotiation is established only on auto-link negotiation with supported link partners. Syntax: default interface <INTERFACE-ID>.
For 6200 AOS-CX 10.06, what is the syntax of the default interface <INTERFACE-ID> command?
Syntax: default interface <INTERFACE-ID>
For 6200 AOS-CX 10.06, what does the interface command do?

The interface command switches to the config-if context for a physical port. This is where you define the configuration settings for the logical interface associated with the physical port. Syntax: interface <PORT-NUM>.
For 6200 AOS-CX 10.06, what is the syntax of the interface command?
Syntax: interface <PORT-NUM>
For 6200 AOS-CX 10.06, what does the interface loopback command do?

The interface loopback command creates a loopback interface and changes to the config-loopback-if context. Loopback interfaces are layer 3. The no form of this command deletes a loopback interface. Syntax: interface loopback <ID>.
For 6200 AOS-CX 10.06, what is the syntax of the interface loopback command?
Syntax: interface loopback <ID>
For 6200 AOS-CX 10.06, what does the no interface vlan <VLAN-ID> command do?

The no interface vlan <VLAN-ID> command creates an interface VLAN also know as an SVI (switched virtual interface) and changes to the config-if- vlan context. The specified VLAN must already be defined on the switch. The no form of this command deletes an interface VLAN.
For 6200 AOS-CX 10.06, what does the ip address command do?

The ip address command sets an IPv4 address for the current layer 3 interface. The no form of this command removes the IPv4 address from the interface. Syntax: ip address <IPv4-ADDR>/<MASK> [secondary].
For 6200 AOS-CX 10.06, what is the syntax of the ip address command?
Syntax: ip address <IPv4-ADDR>/<MASK> [secondary]
For 6200 AOS-CX 10.06, what does the ip mtu command do?

The ip mtu command sets the IP MTU (maximum transmission unit) for an interface. This defines the largest IP packet that can be sent or received by the interface. The no form of this command sets the IP MTU to the default value 1500. Syntax: ip mtu <VALUE>.
For 6200 AOS-CX 10.06, what is the syntax of the ip mtu command?
Syntax: ip mtu <VALUE>
For 6200 AOS-CX 10.06, what is the syntax of the IPv6 address command?
Syntax: IPv6 address <IPv6-ADDR>/<MASK>{eui64 | [tag <ID>]}
For 6200 AOS-CX 10.06, what does the show allow-unsupported-transceiver command do?
The show allow-unsupported-transceiver command displays configuration and status of unsupported transceivers. Syntax: show allow-unsupported-transceiver.
For 6200 AOS-CX 10.06, what is the syntax of the show allow-unsupported-transceiver command?
Syntax: show allow-unsupported-transceiver
For 6200 AOS-CX 10.06, what is the syntax of the show interface command?
Syntax: show interface [<IFNNAME>|<IFRANGE>] [brief | physical | extended [non-zero]]
For 6200 AOS-CX 10.06, what does the show interface dom command do?

The show interface dom command shows diagnostics information and alarm/warning flags for the optical transceivers (SFP, SFP+, QSFP+). This information is known as DOM (Digital Optical Monitoring). DOM information also consists of vendor determined thresholds which trigger high/low alarms and warning flags. Syntax: show interface [<INTERFACE-ID>] dom [detail] [vsx-peer].
For 6200 AOS-CX 10.06, what is the syntax of the show interface dom command?
Syntax: show interface [<INTERFACE-ID>] dom [detail] [vsx-peer]
For 6200 AOS-CX 10.06, what does the show interface transceiver command do?

The show interface transceiver command displays information about transceivers present in the switch. The information shown varies for different transceiver types and manufacturers. Only basic information is shown for unsupported HPE and third-party transceivers installed in the switch and they are also identified with an asterisk in the output. Syntax: show interface [<INTERFACE-ID>] transceiver [detail | threshold-violations] [vsx-peer].
For 6200 AOS-CX 10.06, what is the syntax of the show interface transceiver command?
Syntax: show interface [<INTERFACE-ID>] transceiver [detail | threshold-violations] [vsx-peer]
For 6200 AOS-CX 10.06, what does the show ip interface command do?
The show ip interface command shows status and configuration information for an IPv4 interface. Syntax: show ip interface <INTERFACE-ID> [vsx-peer].
For 6200 AOS-CX 10.06, what is the syntax of the show ip interface command?
Syntax: show ip interface <INTERFACE-ID> [vsx-peer]
For 6200 AOS-CX 10.06, what is the syntax of the show ip source-interface command?
Syntax: show ip source-interface {sflow | tftp | radius | tacacs | all}

syntax: show ip source-interface {sflow | tftp | radius | tacacs | all}

messages.content: For 6200 AOS-CX 10.06, what does the show IPv6 interface command do?
The show IPv6 interface command shows status and configuration information for an IPv6 interface. Syntax: show IPv6 interface <INTERFACE-ID> [vsx-peer].
For 6200 AOS-CX 10.06, what is the syntax of the show IPv6 interface command?
Syntax: show IPv6 interface <INTERFACE-ID> [vsx-peer]
For 6200 AOS-CX 10.06, what is the syntax of the show IPv6 source-interface command?
Syntax: show IPv6 source-interface {sflow | tftp | radius | tacacs | all}

syntax: show IPv6 source-interface {sflow | tftp | radius | tacacs | all}

messages.content: For 6200 AOS-CX 10.06, what does the shutdown command do?
The shutdown command disables an interface. Interfaces are disabled by default when created. The no form of this command enables an interface. Syntax: shutdown.
For 6200 AOS-CX 10.06, what is the syntax of the shutdown command?
Syntax: shutdown
For 6200 AOS-CX 10.06, what does the guide say about VLANs?

VLANs: are primarily used to provide network segmentation at layer 2. VLANs enable the grouping of users by logical function instead of physical location. They make managing bandwidth usage within networks possible by: • Allowing grouping of high-bandwidth users on low-traffic segments • Organizing users from different LAN segments according to their need for common resources and individual protocols • Improving traffic control at the edge of networks by separating traffic of different protocol types. • Enhancing network security by creating subnets to control in-band access to specific network resources VLANs are generally assigned on an organizational basis rather than on a physical basis. For example, a network administrator could assign all workstations and servers used by a particular workgroup to the same VLAN, regardless of their physical locations. Hosts in the same VLAN can directly communicate with one another. A router or a Layer 3 switch is required for hosts in different VLANs to communicate with one another.
For 6200 AOS-CX 10.06, what does the guide say about VLAN interfaces?

VLAN interfaces: Access interface An access interface carries traffic for a single VLAN ID. Access interfaces are generally used to connect end devices that do not support VLANs to the network. The devices connected to an access interface are not aware of the VLAN. Access interface can carry traffic on only one VLAN, either tagged or untagged. Example This example shows ingress and egress traffic behavior for an access interface.
For 6200 AOS-CX 10.06, how do you disable a VLAN?

To disable a VLAN, follow the documented procedure: 1. Switch to configuration context with the command config 2. Switch to configuration context for the VLAN you want to disable with the command vlan 3. Disable the VLAN with the command shutdown.

section: Assigning a VLAN to an interface

messages.content: For 6200 AOS-CX 10.06, what does the guide say about Assigning a VLAN to an interface?

Assigning a VLAN to an interface: To use a VLAN, it must be assigned to an interface on the switch. VLANs can only be assigned to non-routed (layer 2) interfaces. All interfaces are routed (layer 3) by default when created. Use the no routing command to disable routing on an interface.

topic: Assigning a VLAN to an interface

messages.content: For 6200 AOS-CX 10.06, what does the show capacities svi-count command do?
The show capacities svi-count command shows the maximum number of SVIs supported by the switch. Syntax: show capacities svi-count.
For 6200 AOS-CX 10.06, what is the syntax of the show capacities svi-count command?
Syntax: show capacities svi-count
For 6200 AOS-CX 10.06, what does the show vlan [<VLAN-ID>] [vsx-peer] command do?
The show vlan [<VLAN-ID>] [vsx-peer] command displays configuration information for all VLANs or a specific VLAN.
For 6200 AOS-CX 10.06, what does the show VLANsummary command do?
The show VLAN summary command displays a summary of the VLAN configuration on the switch. Syntax: show vlan summary [vsx-peer].
For 6200 AOS-CX 10.06, what is the syntax of the show VLANsummary command?
Syntax: show vlan summary [vsx-peer]
For 6200 AOS-CX 10.06, what does the no vlan <VLAN-LIST> command do?

The no vlan <VLAN-LIST> command creates a VLAN and changes to the config-vlan-id context for the VLAN. By default, the VLAN is enabled. To disable a VLAN, use the no shutdown command. If the specified VLAN exists, this command changes to the config-vlan-id context for the VLAN. If a range of VLANs is specified, the context does not change. The no form of this command removes a VLAN. VLAN 1 is the default VLAN and cannot be deleted.
For 6200 AOS-CX 10.06, what does the no vlan access [<VLAN-ID>] command do?

The no vlan access [<VLAN-ID>] command creates an access interface and assigns an VLAN ID to it. Only one VLAN ID can be assigned to each access interface. VLANs can only be assigned to a non-routed (layer 2) interface or LAG interface. By default, all interfaces are routed (layer 3) when created. Use the no routing command to disable routing on an interface and change the interface to a layer 2 interface. The no form of this command removes an access VLAN from the interface in the current context and sets it to the default VLAN ID of 1.
For 6200 AOS-CX 10.06, what does the no vlan trunk allowed [<VLAN-LIST>] command do?

The no vlan trunk allowed [<VLAN-LIST>] command assigns a VLAN ID to an trunk interface. Multiple VLAN IDs can be assigned to a trunk interface. These VLAN IDs define which VLAN traffic is allowed across the trunk interface. VLANs can be assigned only to a non-routed (layer 2) interface or LAG interface. By default, all interfaces are routed (layer 3) when created. Use the no routing command to disable routing on an interface. The no form of this command removes one or more VLAN IDs from a trunk interface. When the last VLAN is removed from a trunk interface, the interface continues to operate in trunk mode, and will trunk all the VLANs currently defined on the switch, and any new VLANs defined in the future. To disable the trunk interface, use the command shutdown.
For 6200 AOS-CX 10.06, what does the no vlan trunk native [<VLAN-ID>] command do?

The no vlan trunk native [<VLAN-ID>] command assigns a native VLAN ID to a trunk interface. By default, VLAN ID 1 is assigned as the native VLAN ID for all trunk interfaces. VLANs can only be assigned to a non-routed (layer 2) interface or LAG interface. Only one VLAN ID can be assigned as the native VLAN. NOTE: When a native VLAN is defined, the switch automatically executes the vlan trunk allowed all command to ensure that the default VLAN is allowed on the trunk. To only allow specific VLANs on the trunk, issue the vlan trunk allowed command specifying only specific VLANs. The no form of this command removes a native VLAN from a trunk interface and assigns VLAN ID 1 as its native VLAN.
For 6200 AOS-CX 10.06, what does the no vlan trunk native <VLAN-ID> tag command do?

The no vlan trunk native <VLAN-ID> tag command enables tagging on a native VLAN. Only incoming packets that are tagged with the matching VLAN ID are accepted. Incoming packets that are untagged are dropped except for BPDUs. Egress packets are tagged. The no form of this command removes tagging on a native VLAN.
For 6200 AOS-CX 10.06, what does the voice command do?
The voice command configures a VLAN as a voice VLAN. The no form of this command removes voice configuration from a VLAN. Syntax: voice.
For 6200 AOS-CX 10.06, what is the syntax of the voice command?
Syntax: voice
For 6200 AOS-CX 10.06, what does the guide say about Configuration and firmware management?

Configuration and firmware management: Checkpoints A checkpoint is a snapshot of the running configuration of a switch and its relevant metadata during the time of creation. Checkpoints can be used to apply the switch configuration stored within a checkpoint whenever needed, such as to revert to a previous, clean configuration. Checkpoints can be applied to other switches of the same platform. A switch is able to store multiple checkpoints. Checkpoint types The switch supports two types of checkpoints: • System generated checkpoints: The switch automatically generates a system checkpoint whenever a configuration change occurs. • User generated checkpoints: The administrator can manually generate a checkpoint whenever required. Maximum number of checkpoints • Maximum checkpoints: 64 (including the startup configuration) • Maximum user checkpoints: 32 • Maximum system checkpoints: 32 User generated checkpoints User checkpoints can be created at any time, as long as one configuration difference exists since the last checkpoint was created.
For 6200 AOS-CX 10.06, what does the checkpoint auto command do?

The checkpoint auto command starts auto checkpoint mode. In auto checkpoint mode, the switch temporarily saves the runtime configuration as a checkpoint only for the specified time lapse interval. Configuration changes must be saved before the interval expires, otherwise the runtime configuration is restored from the temporary checkpoint. Syntax: checkpoint auto <TIME-LAPSE-INTERVAL>.
For 6200 AOS-CX 10.06, what is the syntax of the checkpoint auto command?
Syntax: checkpoint auto <TIME-LAPSE-INTERVAL>
For 6200 AOS-CX 10.06, what does the checkpoint auto confirm command do?

The checkpoint auto confirm command signals to the switch to save the running configuration used during the auto checkpoint mode. This command also ends the auto checkpoint mode. Syntax: checkpoint auto confirm.
For 6200 AOS-CX 10.06, what is the syntax of the checkpoint auto confirm command?
Syntax: checkpoint auto confirm
For 6200 AOS-CX 10.06, what does the checkpoint diff command do?

The checkpoint diff command shows the difference in configuration between two configurations. Compare checkpoints, the running configuration, or the startup configuration. Syntax: checkpoint diff {<CHECKPOINT-NAME1> | running-config | startup-config}.
For 6200 AOS-CX 10.06, what is the syntax of the checkpoint diff command?
Syntax: checkpoint diff {<CHECKPOINT-NAME1> | running-config | startup-config}
For 6200 AOS-CX 10.06, what does the checkpoint post-configuration timeout command do?
The checkpoint post-configuration timeout command is documented in this guide. Syntax: checkpoint post-configuration timeout <TIMEOUT>.
For 6200 AOS-CX 10.06, what is the syntax of the checkpoint post-configuration timeout command?
Syntax: checkpoint post-configuration timeout <TIMEOUT>
For 6200 AOS-CX 10.06, what does the checkpoint rename command do?
The checkpoint rename command renames an existing checkpoint. Syntax: checkpoint rename <OLD-CHECKPOINT-NAME> <NEW-CHECKPOINT-NAME>.
For 6200 AOS-CX 10.06, what is the syntax of the checkpoint rename command?
Syntax: checkpoint rename <OLD-CHECKPOINT-NAME> <NEW-CHECKPOINT-NAME>
For 6200 AOS-CX 10.06, what does the checkpoint rollback command do?

The checkpoint rollback command applies the configuration from a pre-existing checkpoint or the startup configuration to the running configuration. Syntax: checkpoint rollback {<CHECKPOINT-NAME> | startup-config}.
For 6200 AOS-CX 10.06, what is the syntax of the checkpoint rollback command?
Syntax: checkpoint rollback {<CHECKPOINT-NAME> | startup-config}
For 6200 AOS-CX 10.06, what does the copy checkpoint <CHECKPOINT-NAME> <REMOTE-URL> command do?

The copy checkpoint <CHECKPOINT-NAME> <REMOTE-URL> command copies a checkpoint configuration to a remote location as a file. The configuration is exported in checkpoint format, which includes switch configuration and relevant metadata. Syntax: copy checkpoint <CHECKPOINT-NAME> <REMOTE-URL> [vrf <VRF-NAME>].
For 6200 AOS-CX 10.06, what is the syntax of the copy checkpoint <CHECKPOINT-NAME> <REMOTE-URL> command?
Syntax: copy checkpoint <CHECKPOINT-NAME> <REMOTE-URL> [vrf <VRF-NAME>]
For 6200 AOS-CX 10.06, what is the syntax of the copy checkpoint <CHECKPOINT-NAME> {running-config startup-config} command?
Syntax: copy checkpoint <CHECKPOINT-NAME> {running-config | startup-config}
For 6200 AOS-CX 10.06, what does the copy checkpoint <CHECKPOINT-NAME> <STORAGE-URL> command do?

The copy checkpoint <CHECKPOINT-NAME> <STORAGE-URL> command copies an existing checkpoint configuration to a USB drive. The file format is defined when the checkpoint was created. Syntax: copy checkpoint <CHECKPOINT-NAME> <STORAGE-URL>.
For 6200 AOS-CX 10.06, what is the syntax of the copy checkpoint <CHECKPOINT-NAME> <STORAGE-URL> command?

For 6200 AOS-CX 10.06, what does the guide say about Dynamic segmentation?

Dynamic segmentation: (DS) is an enterprise network solution that combines Aruba OS-CX security and networking features to dynamically place clients into network segments based on client credentials. The client network segments are dynamically carved out of the enterprise networks when on-boarding secure clients. Two options are available: • User Based Tunnels (UBT). • Virtual Network Based Tunnels (VNBT). Also called switch-to-switch dynamic segmentation. In both solutions, once authenticated (using MAC-Auth or 802.1X) an enterprise client is bound to a network role and a VLAN is associated with the role. User traffic is then placed on the VLAN (know as the role VLAN) corresponding to the role to which the user belongs. Role association is defined using the individual client authentication mode or using device-profile based authentication. The administrator must pre-configure all potential role VLANs and VRFs in all access switches (and additional configuration such as IGMP snooping on VLAN, PIM RP, etc.).
For 6200 AOS-CX 10.06, what does the guide say about Aruba Clear Pass Policy Manager?

Aruba Clear Pass Policy Manager: Clear Pass assigns enforcement policies and profiles containing user role information based on profiled devices or authenticated user information. How it works When first configuring the switch, the tunneling profile must be configured first. This is done using the command ubt zone. Within this context, the primary controller IP address can be configured, which should be the physical IP of one of the cluster members. Once the controller information is known on the switch and the UBT service is enabled, the switch then performs a handshake with the controller to determine its reachability and to discover the version information. When reachability is confirmed, the switch executes a switch bootstrap, and sends a bootstrap message to the controller, similar to an AP Hello between an AP and a controller. This bootstrap control packet contains user role information. Once the controller receives the message, it replies with an acknowledge message.

VLAN assignment by switch: Supports only untagged UBT users Supports both tagged/untagged UBT users Controller replicates the broadcast/multicast traffic (converting bcast/mcast to unicast) and sends it to every UBT client Single dedicated multicast GRE tunnel will be established between the controller and switch for Broadcast/Multicast traffic Controller forwards all broadcast/multicast traffic to.

The ubt-mode vlan-extend command selects VLAN extended mode. When VLAN-extend mode is enabled clients are assigned to their UBT role- based VLAN in the hardware datapath. The no form of the command selects local-VLAN mode. In local-VLAN mode clients are assigned to a local switch VLAN and associated with their UBT role-based VLAN when client traffic reaches the controller. The default UBT mode is local-VLAN. Only the SNMP MIB objects that resolve to an ASN.1 primitive type of INTEGER (INTEGER, Integer32, Counter32, Counter64, Gauge32, or Time Ticks) will be monitored. The no form of this command removes all RMON alarms and allows you to specify an index to remove a particular RMON alarm. For 6200 AOS-CX 10.06, what does the guide say about Aruba Central Integration?

Aruba Central Integration: The Aruba Central network management solution, a software-as-a-service subscription in the cloud, provides streamlined management of multiple network devices. Arub a OS-CX switches are able to talk to Aruba Central and utilize cloud-based management functionality. Cloud-based management functionality allows for the deployment of network devices at sites with no or few dedicated IT personnel (branch offices, retail stores, and so forth). Arub a OS-CX switches utilize secure communication protocols to connect to the Aruba Central cloud portal, and can coexist with corporate security standards, such as those mandating the use of firewalls. This feature provides: • Zero-touch provisioning.

When the connection is disabled, the switch does not attempt to connect to the Aruba Central server or fetch central location from any of the three sources (CLI/Aruba Activate/DHCP). It also disconnects any active connection to the Aruba Central server. 184 AOS-CX 10.06 Fundamentals Guide The no form of this command enables connection to the Aruba Central server. The no disable command is identical to the enable command. Enables connection to Aruba Central server. Syntax: aruba-central.

For 6200 AOS-CX 10.06, what is DNS?

DNS: The Domain Name System (DNS) is the Internet protocol for mapping a hostname to its IP address. DNS allows users to enter more readily memorable and intuitive hostnames, rather than IP addresses, to identify devices connected to a network. It also allows a host to keep the same hostname even if it changes its IP address. Hostname resolution can be either static or dynamic. • In static resolution, a local table is defined on the switch that associates hostnames with their IP addresses. Static tables can be used to speed up the resolution of frequently queried hosts. • Dynamic resolution requires that the switch query a DNS server located elsewhere on the network. Dynamic name resolution takes more time than static name resolution, but requires far less configuration and management. DNS client The DNS client resolves hostnames to IP addresses for protocols that are running on the switch. When the DNS client receives a request to resolve a hostname, it can do so in one of two ways: • Forward the request to a DNS name server for resolution.
For 6200 AOS-CX 10.06, how do you configure the DNS client?

To configure the DNS client, follow the documented procedure: 1. Configure one or more DNS name servers with the command ip dns server 2. To resolve DNS requests by appending a domain name to the requests, either configure a single domain 3. To use static name resolution for certain hosts, associate an IP address to a host with the command ip 4. Review your DNS configuration settings with the command show ip dns.
For 6200 AOS-CX 10.06, what does the ip dns domain-list command do?

The ip dns domain-list command configures one or more domain names that are appended to the DNS request. The DNS client appends each name in succession until the DNS server replies. Domains can be either IPv4 or IPv6. By default, requests are forwarded on the default VRF. The no form of this command removes a domain from the list. Syntax: ip dns domain-list <DOMAIN-NAME> [vrf <VRF-NAME>].

If a domain list is defined with the command ip dns domain-list, the domain name defined with this command is ignored. The no form of this command removes the domain name. Syntax: ip dns domain-name <DOMAIN-NAME> [ vrf <VRF-NAME> ].
