For 6200 AOS-CX 10.06, what does the guide say about Device discovery and configuration?

Device discovery and configuration: Device profiles NOTE: Device profiles rely on role configurations. For information on role configurations, see the Security Guide. Device profiles are used to dynamically assign port attributes based on the type of devices connected, without having to create a RADIUS infrastructure. You can map device profiles to device groups. A device group contains various match criteria, which can be obtained from multiple sources, such as LLDP, CDP, and local MAC match. Device profiles contain port attributes to be assigned to the port when a connected device matches a device group. Device profiles are supported on different scenarios. It can be applied on interfaces that are configured with security (802.1X or MAC authentication), or applied based on L2 port (LLDP, CDP), or applied on standalone ports with the block-until-profile-applied command enabled. All the methods are mutually exclusive of each other.

section: Configuring a device profile for LLDP

messages.content: For 6200 AOS-CX 10.06, how do you configure a device profile for LLDP?

To configure a device profile for LLDP, follow the documented procedure: 1. Create an LLDP group with the command port-access lldp-group 2. Define rules for adding devices to an LLDP group with the command match 3. Define rules for ignoring devices so that they are not added to an LLDP group with the command 4. Create a device profile with the command port-access device-profile 5. Add the LLDP group with the command associate lldp-group 6. Add a role to a device profile with the command associate role. Make sure that the role is already 7. Enable the device profile with the command enable.

task: Configuring a device profile for LLDP

section: Configuring a device profile for CDP

messages.content: For 6200 AOS-CX 10.06, how do you configure a device profile for CDP?

To configure a device profile for CDP, follow the documented procedure: 1. Create a CDP group with the command port-access cdp-group 2. Define rules for adding devices to a CDP group with the command match 3. Define rules for ignoring devices so that they are not added to a CDP group with the command ignore 4. Create a device profile with the command port-access device-profile 5. Add a CDP group to a device profile with the command associate cdp-group 6. Add a role to a device profile with the command associate role. Make sure that the role is already 7. Enable a device profile with the command enable.

task: Configuring a device profile for CDP

messages.content: For 6200 AOS-CX 10.06, what does the aaa authentication port-access allow-lldp-bpdu command do?

The aaa authentication port-access allow-lldp-bpdu command allows all packets related to the LLDP BPDU (Bridge Protocol Data Unit) on a secure port. The no form of this command blocks the LLDP BPDU on a secure port. On a nonsecure port, the command has no effect. 200 AOS-CX 10.06 Fundamentals Guide. Syntax: aaa authentication port-access allow-lldp-bpdu.
For 6200 AOS-CX 10.06, what is the syntax of the aaa authentication port-access allow-lldp-bpdu command?
Syntax: aaa authentication port-access allow-lldp-bpdu
For 6200 AOS-CX 10.06, what does the associate cdp-group <GROUP-NAME> command do?

The associate cdp-group <GROUP-NAME> command associates a CDP (Cisco Discovery Protocol) group with a device profile. A maximum of two CDP groups can be associated with a device profile. The no form of this command removes a CDP group from a device profile. Associates an LLDP group with a device profile. A maximum of two LLDP groups can be associated with a device profile The no form of this command removes an LLDP group from a device profile. Associates a MAC group with a device profile. A maximum of two MAC groups can be associated with a device profile. The no form of this command removes a MAC group from a device profile. Syntax: associate cdp-group <GROUP-NAME>.
For 6200 AOS-CX 10.06, what is the syntax of the associate cdp-group <GROUP-NAME> command?
Syntax: associate cdp-group <GROUP-NAME>
For 6200 AOS-CX 10.06, what does the associate role <ROLE-NAME> command do?

The associate role <ROLE-NAME> command associates a role with a device profile. Only one role can be associated with a device profile. For information on how to configure a role, see the port access role information in the Security Guide. The no form of this command removes a role from a device profile. Disables a device profile. The no form of this command enables a device profile. Enables a device profile. The no form of this command disables a device profile. Syntax: associate role <ROLE-NAME>.
For 6200 AOS-CX 10.06, what is the syntax of the associate role <ROLE-NAME> command?
Syntax: associate role <ROLE-NAME>
For 6200 AOS-CX 10.06, what does the mac-group <MAC-GROUP-NAME> command do?

The mac-group <MAC-GROUP-NAME> command creates a MAC group or modifies an existing MAC group. A MAC group is used to classify connected devices based on the MAC address details, such as mask or OUI. A maximum of 32 MAC groups can be configured on the switch. A maximum of 2 MAC groups can be associated with a device profile. Each group accepts 64 match or ignore commands. The no form of this command removes a MAC group. Syntax: mac-group <MAC-GROUP-NAME>.
For 6200 AOS-CX 10.06, what is the syntax of the mac-group <MAC-GROUP-NAME> command?
Syntax: mac-group <MAC-GROUP-NAME>
For 6200 AOS-CX 10.06, what does the port-access cdp-group <CDP-GROUP-NAME> command do?

The port-access cdp-group <CDP-GROUP-NAME> command creates a CDP (Cisco Discovery Protocol) group or modifies an existing CDP group. A CDP Group is used to classify connected devices based on the CDP packet details advertised by the device. A maximum of 32 CDP groups can be configured on the switch. Each group accepts 64 match/ignore commands. The no form of this command removes a CDP group. Creates a new device profile and switches to the config-device-profile context. A maximum of 32 device profiles can be created. The no form of this command removes a device profile. Configures the switch to block the port until a profile match occurs for a device. This configuration is required when no security feature is enabled on the port. You must enable this mode or security on the port for local MAC match feature to operate. Syntax: port-access cdp-group <CDP-GROUP-NAME>.
For 6200 AOS-CX 10.06, what is the syntax of the port-access cdp-group <CDP-GROUP-NAME> command?
Syntax: port-access cdp-group <CDP-GROUP-NAME>
For 6200 AOS-CX 10.06, how do you configure the LLDP agent?

To configure the LLDP agent, follow the documented procedure: 1. By default, the LLDP agent sets the management address in all TLVs in the following order: 2. By default, all supported TLVs are sent and received. To customize the list, use the command lldp. Commands shown in the procedure include: IP address of the loopback interface..
For 6200 AOS-CX 10.06, what does the clear lldp neighbors command do?
The clear lldp neighbors command clears all LLDP neighbor details. Syntax: clear lldp neighbors.
For 6200 AOS-CX 10.06, what is the syntax of the clear lldp neighbors command?
Syntax: clear lldp neighbors
For 6200 AOS-CX 10.06, what does the clear lldp statistics command do?

The clear lldp statistics command clears all LLDP neighbor statistics. 226 AOS-CX 10.06 Fundamentals Guide Enables LLDP support globally on all active interfaces. By default, LLDP is enabled. The no form of this command disables LLDP support globally on all active interfaces. It does not remove any LLDP configuration settings. Sets the 802.3 TLVs to be advertised. By default, advertisement of both POE and MAC/PHY TLVs is enabled. Not supported on the OOBM interface. The no form of this command disables advertisement of 802.3 TLVs. Syntax: clear lldp statistics.
For 6200 AOS-CX 10.06, what is the syntax of the clear lldp statistics command?
Syntax: clear lldp statistics
For 6200 AOS-CX 10.06, what does the lldp holdtime <TIME> command do?

The lldp holdtime <TIME> command sets the holdtime that is used to calculate the LLDP Time-to-Live value. Time-to-Live defines the length of time that neighbors consider LLDP information sent by this agent as valid. When Time-to-Live expires, the information is deleted by the neighbor. Time-to-live is calculated by multiplying holdtime by the value of lldp timer. The no form of this command sets the holdtime to its default value of 4. Defines the IPv4 management address of the switch which is sent in the management address TLV. One IPv4 and one IPv6 management address can be configured. Syntax: lldp holdtime <TIME>.
For 6200 AOS-CX 10.06, what is the syntax of the lldp holdtime <TIME> command?
Syntax: lldp holdtime <TIME>
For 6200 AOS-CX 10.06, what does the lldp med [poe [priority-override] | capability | network-policy] command do?

The lldp med [poe [priority-override] | capability | network-policy] command defines the IPv6 management address of the switch. The management address is encapsulated in the management address TLV. If no management address is specified, LLDP uses the IP address of the management interface, and if this the management interface is not defined, the chassis ID. The no form of this command removes the IPv6 management address of the switch. Configures support for the LLDP-MED TLV. LLDP-MED (media endpoint devices) is an extension to LLDP developed by TIA to support interoperability between VoIP endpoint devices and other networking end- devices. The switch only sends the LLDP MED TLV after receiving a MED TLV from and connected endpoint device. Not supported on the OOBM interface. The no form of this command disables support for the LLDP MED TLV. Syntax: lldp med [poe [priority-override] | capability | network-policy].
For 6200 AOS-CX 10.06, what is the syntax of the lldp med [poe [priority-override] | capability | network-policy] command?
Syntax: lldp med [poe [priority-override] | capability | network-policy]
For 6200 AOS-CX 10.06, what does the lldp receive command do?

The lldp receive command enables reception of LLDP information on an interface. By default, LLDP reception is enabled on all active interfaces, including the OOBM interface. The no form of this command disables reception of LLDP information on an interface. Sets the amount of time (in seconds) to wait before performing LLDP initialization on an interface. The no form of this command sets the reinitialization time to its default value of 2 seconds. Selects a TLV that the LLDP agent will send and receive. By default, all supported TLVs are sent and received. The no form of this command stops the LLDP agent from sending and receiving a specific TLV. Syntax: lldp receive.
For 6200 AOS-CX 10.06, what is the syntax of the lldp receive command?
Syntax: lldp receive
For 6200 AOS-CX 10.06, what does the lldp timer <TIME> command do?

The lldp timer <TIME> command sets the interval (in seconds) at which local LLDP information is updated and TLVs are sent to neighboring network devices by the LLDP agent. The minimum setting for this timer must be four times the value of lldp txdelay. For example, this is a valid configuration: • lldp timer = 16 • lldp txdelay = 4 And, this is an invalid configuration: 234 AOS-CX 10.06 Fundamentals Guide • lldp timer = 5 • lldp txdelay = 2 NOTE: When copying a saved configuration to the running configuration, the value for lldp timer is applied before the value of lldp txdelay. This can result in a configuration error if the saved configuration has a value of lldp timer that is not four times the value of lldp txdelay in the running configuration. Syntax: lldp timer <TIME>.
For 6200 AOS-CX 10.06, what is the syntax of the lldp timer <TIME> command?
Syntax: lldp timer <TIME>
For 6200 AOS-CX 10.06, what does the show lldp tlv command do?
The show lldp tlv command shows the LLDP TLVs that are configured for send and receive. Syntax: show lldp tlv [vsx-peer].
For 6200 AOS-CX 10.06, what is the syntax of the show lldp tlv command?
Syntax: show lldp tlv [vsx-peer]
For 6200 AOS-CX 10.06, what does the show lldp statistics mgmt command do?
The show lldp statistics mgmt command shows LLDP statistics for the OOBM interface. Syntax: show lldp statistics mgmt.
For 6200 AOS-CX 10.06, what is the syntax of the show lldp statistics mgmt command?
Syntax: show lldp statistics mgmt
For 6200 AOS-CX 10.06, what does the show lldp neighbor-info command do?

The show lldp neighbor-info command displays information about neighboring devices for all interfaces or for a specific interface. The information displayed varies depending on the type of neighbor connected and the type of TLVs sent by the neighbor. Syntax: show lldp neighbor-info [<INTERFACE-NAME>] [vsx-peer].
For 6200 AOS-CX 10.06, what is the syntax of the show lldp neighbor-info command?
Syntax: show lldp neighbor-info [<INTERFACE-NAME>] [vsx-peer]
For 6200 AOS-CX 10.06, what does the show lldp neighbor-info detail command do?
The show lldp neighbor-info detail command shows detailed LLDP neighbor information for all LLDP neighbor connected interfaces. Syntax: show lldp neighbor-info detail [vsx-peer].
For 6200 AOS-CX 10.06, what is the syntax of the show lldp neighbor-info detail command?
Syntax: show lldp neighbor-info detail [vsx-peer]
For 6200 AOS-CX 10.06, what does the show lldp neighbor-info mgmt command do?
The show lldp neighbor-info mgmt command displays information about neighboring devices connected to the OOBM interface. Syntax: show lldp neighbor-info mgmt.
For 6200 AOS-CX 10.06, what is the syntax of the show lldp neighbor-info mgmt command?
Syntax: show lldp neighbor-info mgmt
For 6200 AOS-CX 10.06, what does the show lldp local-device command do?

The show lldp local-device command shows global LLDP information advertised by the switch, as well as port-based data. If VLANs are configured on any active interfaces, the VLAN ID is only shown for trunk native or untagged VLAN IDs on access interfaces. Syntax: show lldp local-device [vsx-peer].
For 6200 AOS-CX 10.06, what is the syntax of the show lldp local-device command?
Syntax: show lldp local-device [vsx-peer]
For 6200 AOS-CX 10.06, what does the guide say about Parent Interface: interface 1/1/11?

Parent Interface: interface 1/1/11: Cisco Discovery Protocol (CDP) Cisco Discovery Protocol (CDP) is a proprietary layer 2 protocol supported by most Cisco devices. It is used to exchange information, such as software version, device capabilities, and voice VLAN information, between directly connected devices, such as a VoIP phone and a switch. CDP support By default, CDP is enabled on each active switch port. This is a read-only capability, which means the switch can receive and store information about adjacent CDP devices, but does not generate CDP packets (except when communicating with Cisco IP phones.) The switch supports CDPv2 only and does not support SNMP MIB traps. When a CDP-enabled port receives a CDP packet from another CDP device, it enters data for that device into the CDP Neighbors table, along with the port number on which the data was received. It does not forward the packet. The switch also periodically purges the table of any entries that have expired.
For 6200 AOS-CX 10.06, what does the clear cdp counters command do?
The clear cdp counters command clears CDP counters. Syntax: clear cdp counters.
For 6200 AOS-CX 10.06, what is the syntax of the clear cdp counters command?
Syntax: clear cdp counters
For 6200 AOS-CX 10.06, what does the clear cdp neighbor-info command do?
The clear cdp neighbor-info command clears CDP neighbor information. Syntax: clear cdp neighbor-info.
For 6200 AOS-CX 10.06, what is the syntax of the clear cdp neighbor-info command?
Syntax: clear cdp neighbor-info
For 6200 AOS-CX 10.06, what does the show cdp command do?
The show cdp command shows CDP information for all interfaces. Syntax: show cdp.
For 6200 AOS-CX 10.06, what is the syntax of the show cdp command?
Syntax: show cdp
For 6200 AOS-CX 10.06, what does the show cdp neighbor-info command do?
The show cdp neighbor-info command shows CDP information for all neighbors or for CDP information on a specific interface. Syntax: show cdp neighbor-info <INTERFACE-ID>.
For 6200 AOS-CX 10.06, what is the syntax of the show cdp neighbor-info command?
Syntax: show cdp neighbor-info <INTERFACE-ID>
For 6200 AOS-CX 10.06, what does the guide say about Zero Touch Provisioning?

Zero Touch Provisioning: (ZTP) enables the auto-configuration of factory default switches without a network administrator onsite. When a switch is booted from its factory default configuration, ZTP autoprovisions the switch by automatically downloading and installing a firmware file, a configuration file, or both. With ZTP, even a nontechnical user (for example: a store manager in a retail chain or a teacher in a school) can deploy devices at a site. ZTP support The switch supports standards-based Zero Touch Provisioning (ZTP) operations as follows: • The switch must be running the factory default configuration. • The switch can connect to the DHCP server from the OOBM management port. The switch can connect to the DHCP server from either the OOBM management port, or a data port on the default VLAN. • ZTP operations are supported over IPv4 connections only. IPv6 connections are not supported for ZTP operations. • You must configure the DHCP server to provide a standards-based ZTP server solution.
For 6200 AOS-CX 10.06, what does the show ztp information command do?
The show ztp information command shows information about Zero Touch Provisioning (ZTP) operations performed on the switch. Syntax: show ztp information.
For 6200 AOS-CX 10.06, what is the syntax of the show ztp information command?
Syntax: show ztp information
For 6200 AOS-CX 10.06, what does the ztp force-provision command do?
The ztp force-provision command starts on-demand ZTP. Syntax: ztp force-provision.
For 6200 AOS-CX 10.06, what is the syntax of the ztp force-provision command?
Syntax: ztp force-provision
For 6200 AOS-CX 10.06, what does the show usb file-system command do?

The show usb file-system command shows directory listings for a mounted USB device. When entered without the <PATH> parameter the top level directory tree is shown. Syntax: show usb file-system [<PATH>].
For 6200 AOS-CX 10.06, what is the syntax of the show usb file-system command?
Syntax: show usb file-system [<PATH>]
For 6200 AOS-CX 10.06, what does the redundancy switchover command do?

The redundancy switchover command causes the switch to immediately switch over to the Standby Management Module. This command must be executed from the Active Management Module and will fail if the Standby Management Module is in a failed state or not present. Syntax: redundancy switchover.
For 6200 AOS-CX 10.06, what is the syntax of the redundancy switchover command?
Syntax: redundancy switchover
For 6200 AOS-CX 10.06, what is BFD?

BFD: NOTE: The BFD feature and thus this entire chapter is not applicable to the 6200 Switch Series. Bidirectional Forwarding Detection (BFD) provides a general-purpose, standard, medium- and protocol- independent fast failure detection mechanism. It can detect and monitor the connectivity of links in IP to detect communication failures quickly. BFD operates independently of media, data protocols, and routing protocols. BFD establishes a session between two network devices to detect failures on the bidirectional forwarding paths between the devices and provide services for upper-layer protocols. BFD provides no neighbor discovery mechanism. Protocols that BFD services notify BFD of devices to which it needs to establish sessions. After a session is established, if no BFD control packet is received from the peer within the negotiated BFD interval, BFD notifies a failure to the protocol, which then takes appropriate measures. BFD operates in two modes: • Asynchronous mode: In this mode, an operating device periodically sends BFD control packets to another device.

section: Configuring BFD for an IPv4 static route

messages.content: For 6200 AOS-CX 10.06, how do you configure BFD for an IPv4 static route?
To configure BFD for an IPv4 static route, follow the documented procedure: 1. Enable BFD support with the command bfd 2. Enable BFD on an IPv4 static route with the command ip route bfd.

task: Configuring BFD for an IPv4 static route

messages.content: For 6200 AOS-CX 10.06, how do you configure BFD For OSPFv2?

To configure BFD For OSPFv2, follow the documented procedure: 1. Enable BFD support with the command bfd 2. Enable BFD on all OSPF interfaces with the command bfd all-interfaces, or enable BFD on a specific. Commands shown in the procedure include: interface with the command ip ospf bfd..
For 6200 AOS-CX 10.06, how do you configure BFD For OSPFv3?

To configure BFD For OSPFv3, follow the documented procedure: 1. Enable BFD support with the command bfd 2. Enable BFD on all OSPF interfaces with the command bfd all-interfaces, or enable BFD on a specific. Commands shown in the procedure include: interface with the command IPv6 ospfv3 bfd..
For 6200 AOS-CX 10.06, how do you configure BFD for VRRP?
To configure BFD for VRRP, follow the documented procedure: 1. Enable BFD support with the command bfd 2. Enable BFD on a VRRP interface with the command bfd <IPv4-ADDR>.
For 6200 AOS-CX 10.06, what does the bfd command do?
The bfd command is documented in this guide. Syntax: bfd.
For 6200 AOS-CX 10.06, what is the syntax of the bfd command?
Syntax: bfd
For 6200 AOS-CX 10.06, what does the bfd <IPv4-ADDR> command do?

The bfd <IPv4-ADDR> command enables BFD under VRRP for the specified IP address. BFD is asynchronous and echo mode is supported. The no form of this command disables BFD under VRRP for the specified IP address. Syntax: bfd <IPv4-ADDR>.
For 6200 AOS-CX 10.06, what is the syntax of the bfd <IPv4-ADDR> command?
Syntax: bfd <IPv4-ADDR>
For 6200 AOS-CX 10.06, what does the bfd all-interfaces command do?

The bfd all-interfaces command enables BFD on all OSPFv2 or OSPFv3 interfaces. The no form of this command disables BFD on all OSPFv2/OSPFv3 or PIM IPv4/IPv6 interfaces, excluding those on which BFD was enabled at the interface level with the commands ip ospf bfd and IPv6 ospfv3 bfd. Syntax: bfd all-interfaces.
For 6200 AOS-CX 10.06, what is the syntax of the bfd all-interfaces command?
Syntax: bfd all-interfaces
For 6200 AOS-CX 10.06, what does the bfd detect-multiplier command do?
The bfd detect-multiplier command is documented in this guide. Syntax: bfd detect-multiplier <MULTIPLIER>.
For 6200 AOS-CX 10.06, what is the syntax of the bfd detect-multiplier command?
Syntax: bfd detect-multiplier <MULTIPLIER>
For 6200 AOS-CX 10.06, what does the bfd disable command do?
The bfd disable command disables BFD on the switch, but retains all configuration settings. Syntax: bfd disable.
For 6200 AOS-CX 10.06, what is the syntax of the bfd disable command?
Syntax: bfd disable
For 6200 AOS-CX 10.06, what does the bfd enable command do?
The bfd enable command enables or disables BFD for HSC feature. Syntax: bfd enable.
For 6200 AOS-CX 10.06, what is the syntax of the bfd enable command?
Syntax: bfd enable
For 6200 AOS-CX 10.06, what does the bfd echo disable command do?
The bfd echo disable command is documented in this guide. Syntax: bfd echo disable.
For 6200 AOS-CX 10.06, what is the syntax of the bfd echo disable command?
Syntax: bfd echo disable
For 6200 AOS-CX 10.06, what does the bfd echo-src-ip-address command do?

The bfd echo-src-ip-address command sets the source IPv4 address for BFD echo packets. This address is used in all echo sessions. NOTE: The source IP address must not be on the same network segment as any switch interface, otherwise a large number of ICMP redirect packets may be sent by the remote device, causing network congestion. The no form of this command removes the source IPv4 address for BFD echo packets, which causes the switch to stop sending echo packets. When a valid value is set, all sessions with a peer that is capable of receiving echo packets, will start transmitting echo packets. BFD control sessions continue to run concurrently with echo packets. 30 AOS-CX 10.06 High Availability Guide. Syntax: bfd echo-src-ip-address <IPv4-ADDR>.
For 6200 AOS-CX 10.06, what is the syntax of the bfd echo-src-ip-address command?
Syntax: bfd echo-src-ip-address <IPv4-ADDR>
For 6200 AOS-CX 10.06, what does the bfd min-echo-receive-interval command do?

The bfd min-echo-receive-interval command sets the minimum time interval between received BFD echo packets. The no form of this command sets the minimum interval between received BFD echo packets to the default value of 500 milliseconds. Syntax: bfd min-echo-receive-interval <INTERVAL>.
For 6200 AOS-CX 10.06, what is the syntax of the bfd min-echo-receive-interval command?
Syntax: bfd min-echo-receive-interval <INTERVAL>
For 6200 AOS-CX 10.06, what does the bfd min-receive-interval command do?

The bfd min-receive-interval command sets the minimum time interval between received BFD control packets on an interface. The no form of this command sets the minimum interval between received BFD control packets to the default value of 3000 milliseconds. Syntax: bfd min-receive-interval <INTERVAL>.
For 6200 AOS-CX 10.06, what is the syntax of the bfd min-receive-interval command?
Syntax: bfd min-receive-interval <INTERVAL>
For 6200 AOS-CX 10.06, what does the bfd min-transmit-interval command do?

The bfd min-transmit-interval command sets the minimum time interval between transmitted BFD control packets on an interface. The no form of this command sets the minimum interval between transmitted BFD control packets to the default value of 3000 ms. Syntax: bfd min-transmit-interval <INTERVAL>.
For 6200 AOS-CX 10.06, what is the syntax of the bfd min-transmit-interval command?
Syntax: bfd min-transmit-interval <INTERVAL>
For 6200 AOS-CX 10.06, what does the clear bfd statistics command do?
The clear bfd statistics command clears statistics for all BFD sessions or for a specific BFD session. Syntax: clear bfd statistics [session <ID>].
For 6200 AOS-CX 10.06, what is the syntax of the clear bfd statistics command?
Syntax: clear bfd statistics [session <ID>]
For 6200 AOS-CX 10.06, what does the ip ospf bfd command do?

The ip ospf bfd command enables BFD for OSPFv2 on the current interface. The interface must have OSPFv2 enabled on it. This overrides the global settings defined with the command bfd all-interfaces. The no form of this command sets the current interface to the global settings defined with the command bfd all-interfaces. Syntax: ip ospf bfd.
For 6200 AOS-CX 10.06, what is the syntax of the ip ospf bfd command?
Syntax: ip ospf bfd
For 6200 AOS-CX 10.06, what does the ip ospf bfd disable command do?
The ip ospf bfd disable command disables BFD for OSPFv2 on the current interface. This overrides the global settings defined with the command bfd all-interfaces. Syntax: ip ospf bfd disable.
For 6200 AOS-CX 10.06, what is the syntax of the ip ospf bfd disable command?
Syntax: ip ospf bfd disable
For 6200 AOS-CX 10.06, what does the ip route bfd command do?

The ip route bfd command enables or disables BFD on the specified static route. To disable BFD, issue the command without the bfd option. Syntax: ip route <DEST-IPv4-ADDR>/<NETMASK> [<NEXT-HOP-IP-ADDR> | <INTERFACE>] [bfd].
For 6200 AOS-CX 10.06, what is the syntax of the ip route bfd command?
Syntax: ip route <DEST-IPv4-ADDR>/<NETMASK> [<NEXT-HOP-IP-ADDR> | <INTERFACE>] [bfd]
For 6200 AOS-CX 10.06, what does the IPv6 ospfv3 bfd command do?

The IPv6 ospfv3 bfd command enables BFD for OSPFv3 on the current interface. The interface must have OSPFv3 enabled on it. This overrides the global settings defined with the command bfd all-interfaces. The no form of this command sets the current interface to the global settings defined with the command bfd all-interfaces. Syntax: IPv6 ospfv3 bfd.
For 6200 AOS-CX 10.06, what is the syntax of the IPv6 ospfv3 bfd command?
Syntax: IPv6 ospfv3 bfd
For 6200 AOS-CX 10.06, what does the IPv6 ospfv3 bfd disable command do?
The IPv6 ospfv3 bfd disable command disables BFD on the current OSPFv3 interface. This overrides the global settings defined with the command bfd all-interfaces. Syntax: IPv6 ospfv3 bfd disable.
For 6200 AOS-CX 10.06, what is the syntax of the IPv6 ospfv3 bfd disable command?
Syntax: IPv6 ospfv3 bfd disable
For 6200 AOS-CX 10.06, what does the neighbor fall-over bfd command do?

The neighbor fall-over bfd command enables BGP to register with BFD to receive fast peering session deactivation messages from BFD. The no form of this command disables BGP for BFD. NOTE: Currently, BFD is supported only with IPv4 neighbors. Support to enable BFD with IPv6 neighbors will be added in a future release. Syntax: neighbor {<IP-ADDRESS>|<PEER-GROUP-NAME>} fall-over bfd.
For 6200 AOS-CX 10.06, what is the syntax of the neighbor fall-over bfd command?
Syntax: neighbor {<IP-ADDRESS>|<PEER-GROUP-NAME>} fall-over bfd
For 6200 AOS-CX 10.06, what does the show bfd command do?
The show bfd command shows information for all BFD sessions or for a specific BFD session. Syntax: show bfd [session <ID>] [all-vrfs | vrf <NAME>] [vsx-peer].
For 6200 AOS-CX 10.06, what is the syntax of the show bfd command?
Syntax: show bfd [session <ID>] [all-vrfs | vrf <NAME>] [vsx-peer]
For 6200 AOS-CX 10.06, what does the show hsc command do?
The show hsc command displays connection information for the remote controller. Syntax: show hsc.
For 6200 AOS-CX 10.06, what is the syntax of the show hsc command?
Syntax: show hsc
For 6200 AOS-CX 10.06, what does the clear erps ring <ringid> instance <id> command do?

The clear erps ring <ringid> instance <id> command removes the protection switching and triggers reversion both in revertive and non-revertive operation. This command will not change the configured revertive operation mode. Syntax: clear erps ring <ringid> instance <id>.
For 6200 AOS-CX 10.06, what is the syntax of the clear erps ring <ringid> instance <id> command?
Syntax: clear erps ring <ringid> instance <id>
For 6200 AOS-CX 10.06, what does the clear erps statistics command do?
The clear erps statistics command this command clears the ERPS statistics for a ring or a ring instance. Syntax: clear erps statistics [ring <id>] [instance <id>].
For 6200 AOS-CX 10.06, what is the syntax of the clear erps statistics command?
Syntax: clear erps statistics [ring <id>] [instance <id>]
For 6200 AOS-CX 10.06, what does the erps ring command do?
The erps ring command this command creates an ERPS ring with a given ID. The no form of this command removes all the configurations of the ring, including instances. Syntax: erps ring <ringid>.
For 6200 AOS-CX 10.06, what is the syntax of the erps ring command?
Syntax: erps ring <ringid>
For 6200 AOS-CX 10.06, what does the erps ring <ringid> <port0 port1> interface command do?

The erps ring <ringid> <port0 port1> interface command this command configures the ERPS ring member port. An L2 interface in the switch is associated to one of the two member ports of an ERPS ring. In case of an interconnection node, only port0 is applicable for the sub-ring. The no form of this command removes the association of the ring port to the L2 interface on the switch. Syntax: erps ring <ringid>.
For 6200 AOS-CX 10.06, what is the syntax of the erps ring <ringid> <port0 port1> interface command?
Syntax: erps ring <ringid>
For 6200 AOS-CX 10.06, what does the erps ring <ringid> description command do?

The erps ring <ringid> description command this command adds descriptive information to help administrators and operators understand the purpose of a ring. 1-64 printable ASCII characters are allowed. Syntax: erps ring <ringid>.
For 6200 AOS-CX 10.06, what is the syntax of the erps ring <ringid> description command?
Syntax: erps ring <ringid>
For 6200 AOS-CX 10.06, what does the erps ring <ringid> guard-interval command do?

The erps ring <ringid> guard-interval command guard timer is used in nodes recovering from a local failure to avoid loops due to earlier Signal Fail (SF) messages that may be in the ring. The configuration specifies the guard timer duration in units of 10 ms. The timer period must be greater than the maximum expected forwarding delay in which an R-APS message traverses the entire ring. The default value is 50. The no form of this command removes the configured value of the guard interval and sets it to the default value of 50. Syntax: erps ring <ringid>.
For 6200 AOS-CX 10.06, what is the syntax of the erps ring <ringid> guard-interval command?
Syntax: erps ring <ringid>
For 6200 AOS-CX 10.06, what does the erps ring <ringid> hold-off-interval command do?

The erps ring <ringid> hold-off-interval command specifies hold-off interval in units of 100 ms. If specified, a defect is not reported immediately. Instead, the hold-off timer is started. On expiration of the timer, if the defect still exists, it is reported to protection switching. The default value for hold-off timer is 0. The no form of this command removes the configured value of the hold-off interval and sets it to the default value of 0. Syntax: erps ring <ringid>.
For 6200 AOS-CX 10.06, what is the syntax of the erps ring <ringid> hold-off-interval command?
Syntax: erps ring <ringid>
For 6200 AOS-CX 10.06, what does the erps ring <ringid> instance command do?

The erps ring <ringid> instance command on a common ERPS network, a physical ring can be configured with a single ERPS ring, and only one blocked port can be specified in the ring. When the ERPS ring is in normal state, the blocked port prohibits all service packets from passing through. As a result, all service data is transmitted through one path over the ERPS ring, and the other link on the blocked port becomes idle, leading to ineffective use of bandwidth. To improve link use efficiency, logical rings can be configured in the same physical ring in the ERPS multi- instance. A port may have different roles in different ERPS rings and different ERPS rings use different control VLANs. An ERPS ring must be configured with an ERP instance, and each ERP instance specifies a range of VLANs. Syntax: erps ring <ringid>.
For 6200 AOS-CX 10.06, what is the syntax of the erps ring <ringid> instance command?
Syntax: erps ring <ringid>
For 6200 AOS-CX 10.06, what does the erps ring <ringid> instance <id> control-vlan command do?

The erps ring <ringid> instance <id> control-vlan command this command adds a control-channel VLAN to a ring instance. In an ERPS ring, the control VLANshould be used only to forward RAPS PDUs and not service packets. All the devices in an ERPS ring instance must be configured with the same control VLAN, and different ERPS ring instances must use different control VLANs. The no form of this command removes the control-channel VLAN of the ring instance. Syntax: erps ring <ringid>.
For 6200 AOS-CX 10.06, what is the syntax of the erps ring <ringid> instance <id> control-vlan command?
Syntax: erps ring <ringid>
For 6200 AOS-CX 10.06, what does the erps ring <ringid> instance <id> description command do?

The erps ring <ringid> instance <id> description command this command adds descriptive information to help administrators and operators understand the purpose of a ring instance. 1-64 printable ASCII characters are allowed. The no form of this command removes the ring instance description. Syntax: erps ring <ringid>.
For 6200 AOS-CX 10.06, what is the syntax of the erps ring <ringid> instance <id> description command?
Syntax: erps ring <ringid>
For 6200 AOS-CX 10.06, what does the erps ring <ringid> instance <id> enable command do?

The erps ring <ringid> instance <id> enable command this configuration enables protection switching on the given instance of the ring. It is disabled by default. The no form of this command disables protection switching on the given instance of the ring. Syntax: erps ring <ringid>.
For 6200 AOS-CX 10.06, what is the syntax of the erps ring <ringid> instance <id> enable command?
Syntax: erps ring <ringid>
For 6200 AOS-CX 10.06, what does the erps ring <ringid> instance <id> protected-vlans command do?

The erps ring <ringid> instance <id> protected-vlans command this command specifies the set of VLANs that are protected by this ring instance. The no form of this command removes a set of VLANs that are protected by this ring instance. Syntax: erps ring <ringid>.
For 6200 AOS-CX 10.06, what is the syntax of the erps ring <ringid> instance <id> protected-vlans command?
Syntax: erps ring <ringid>
For 6200 AOS-CX 10.06, what does the erps ring <ringid> instance <id> protection-switch command do?

The erps ring <ringid> instance <id> protection-switch command blocks a specific ring interface in one of the two following ways: • Force: The switch blocks a specific ring interface regardless of the protection switching state of the ring instance. • Manual: The switch blocks a specific ring interface if no other protection switch event is active on the ring instance. NOTE: The user can verify whether the protection-switch is successful by verifying the status of instance and port state over which this command is executed. Syntax: erps ring <ringid> instance <id> protection-switch {{manual|force} <port0>|<port1>}.
For 6200 AOS-CX 10.06, what is the syntax of the erps ring <ringid> instance <id> protection-switch command?
Syntax: erps ring <ringid> instance <id> protection-switch {{manual|force} <port0>|<port1>}
For 6200 AOS-CX 10.06, what does the erps ring <ringid> instance <id> revertive command do?

The erps ring <ringid> instance <id> revertive command configures the default revertive mode of operation for an ERPS ring. In revertive operation, after the conditions causing protection switching are cleared, traffic channels are restored to the recovered link blocking the RPL. This configuration is meaningful only on the RPL node. The no form of this command configures non-revertive mode of operation for an ERPS ring. In non-revertive operation, the traffic channels continue to use the RPL, if it has not failed, after conditions causing protection switching are cleared. This configuration is meaningful only on the RPL node. Syntax: erps ring <ringid> instance <id> revertive.
For 6200 AOS-CX 10.06, what is the syntax of the erps ring <ringid> instance <id> revertive command?
Syntax: erps ring <ringid> instance <id> revertive
For 6200 AOS-CX 10.06, what does the erps ring <ringid> instance <id> role command do?

The erps ring <ringid> instance <id> role command in ERPS, there is a central node called RPL Owner Node which blocks one of the ports to ensure that there is no loop formed for the Ethernet traffic. The link blocked by the RPL owner node is called the Ring Protection Link or RPL. The node at the other end of the RPL is known as RPL Neighbor Node. It uses R-APS control messages to coordinate the activities of switching on/off the RPL link. This command specifies the role of the node as owner or neighbor. The no form of this command removes the configuration of the node role from the instance. Syntax: erps ring <ringid>.
For 6200 AOS-CX 10.06, what is the syntax of the erps ring <ringid> instance <id> role command?
Syntax: erps ring <ringid>
For 6200 AOS-CX 10.06, what does the erps ring <ringid> instance <id> rpl command do?

The erps ring <ringid> instance <id> rpl command in ERPS, there is a central node called RPL Owner Node which blocks one of the ports to ensure that there is no loop formed for the Ethernet traffic. The link blocked by the RPL owner node is called the Ring Protection Link or RPL. The node at the other end of the RPL is known as RPL Neighbor Node. It uses R-APS control messages to coordinate the activities of switching the RPL link on and off. This command specifies which of the ERPS ring ports is the RPL. The no form of this command removes the RPL port configuration from the ERPS ring instance. Syntax: erps ring <ringid>.
For 6200 AOS-CX 10.06, what is the syntax of the erps ring <ringid> instance <id> rpl command?
Syntax: erps ring <ringid>
For 6200 AOS-CX 10.06, what does the erps ring <ringid> meg-level command do?

The erps ring <ringid> meg-level command the R-APS messages transmitted by ERPS take the form of OAM PDUs as defined in G.8013. Each OAM PDU is transmitted at a specified level known as the Maintenance Entity Group (MEG) level. This command configures the level with which the ERPS packets must be transmitted. The no form of this command removes the configured MEG level and sets it to the default value of 7. Syntax: erps ring <ringid>.
For 6200 AOS-CX 10.06, what is the syntax of the erps ring <ringid> meg-level command?
Syntax: erps ring <ringid>
For 6200 AOS-CX 10.06, what does the erps ring <ringid> parent-ring command do?

The erps ring <ringid> parent-ring command this command associates a sub-ring to a parent-ring and is required for the sub-ring to notify the parent- ring on change in topology. The no form of this command removes the parent ring identifier. 56 AOS-CX 10.06 High Availability Guide. Syntax: erps ring <ringid>.
For 6200 AOS-CX 10.06, what is the syntax of the erps ring <ringid> parent-ring command?
Syntax: erps ring <ringid>
For 6200 AOS-CX 10.06, what does the erps ring <ringid> sub-ring command do?

The erps ring <ringid> sub-ring command this command is to configure a sub-ring. If not specified, the ring is a major-ring. The no form of this command removes the sub-ring configuration of the ring and configures it to be a major-ring. Syntax: erps ring <ringid>.
For 6200 AOS-CX 10.06, what is the syntax of the erps ring <ringid> sub-ring command?
Syntax: erps ring <ringid>
For 6200 AOS-CX 10.06, what does the erps ring <ringid> tcn-propogation command do?

The erps ring <ringid> tcn-propogation command this command is to configure a sub-ring interconnection node to pass a topology change notification to the ring instance for the parent ring whenever the topology of the sub-ring changes. The parent ring instance performs a Forwarding Database (FDB) flush and sends a protocol message to ensure that other nodes on the parent ring also perform an FDB flush. The no form of this command disables topology change notifications. Syntax: erps ring <ringid>.
For 6200 AOS-CX 10.06, what is the syntax of the erps ring <ringid> tcn-propogation command?
Syntax: erps ring <ringid>
For 6200 AOS-CX 10.06, what does the erps ring <ringid> transmission-interval command do?

The erps ring <ringid> transmission-interval command specifies the R-APS periodic transmission interval in units of seconds. Default is 5 seconds. The no form of this command removes the configured value of the transmission interval and sets it to the default value of 5 seconds. 58 AOS-CX 10.06 High Availability Guide. Syntax: erps ring <ringid>.
For 6200 AOS-CX 10.06, what is the syntax of the erps ring <ringid> transmission-interval command?
Syntax: erps ring <ringid>
For 6200 AOS-CX 10.06, what does the erps ring <ringid> wtr-interval command do?

The erps ring <ringid> wtr-interval command the RPL owner node uses a delay timer before initiating an RPL block in case of both revertive mode of operation or before reverting to idle state after clearing operator commands (FS, MS). The Wait to Restore (WTR) timer can be configured in 1-minute increments up to 12 minutes. The default value is 5 minutes. When recovering from an SF, the delay timer must be long enough to allow the recovering network to become stable. In the default revertive mode of operation, the WTR timer is used to prevent frequent operation of protection switching due to intermittent SF defects. The no form of this command removes the configured value of the wtr-interval and sets it to the default value of 5 minutes. Syntax: erps ring <ringid>.
For 6200 AOS-CX 10.06, what is the syntax of the erps ring <ringid> wtr-interval command?
Syntax: erps ring <ringid>
For 6200 AOS-CX 10.06, what does the show erps statistics command do?

The show erps statistics command this command displays ERPS statistics. The statistics can be displayed for the ring, the instance, or the instance ports. Syntax: show erps statistics [ring <ringid>] [instance <id> [port0|port1]].
For 6200 AOS-CX 10.06, what is the syntax of the show erps statistics command?
Syntax: show erps statistics [ring <ringid>] [instance <id> [port0|port1]]
For 6200 AOS-CX 10.06, what does the show erps status command do?

The show erps status command this command displays detailed information about a specific ring or all instances of a ring. The ring instance may be in one of the following states: • • Idle: The ring instance is operational. Initializing: The ring instance is not operational. • Protection: Protection switching has been triggered by a local or remote link failure. • Pending: Pending clearance of a previous protection switch. • Down: Ring instance is not active. • Manual-switch: Manual protection switching triggered by Admin-down. • Force-switch: Forced protection switching triggered by admin. A ring instance has the following reasons for "down" state:. Syntax: show erps status [ring <ringid>] [instance <id>].
For 6200 AOS-CX 10.06, what is the syntax of the show erps status command?
Syntax: show erps status [ring <ringid>] [instance <id>]
For 6200 AOS-CX 10.06, what does the show erps summary command do?
The show erps summary command this command displays a summary of the ERPS configuration and state for the ERPS ring instances. Syntax: show erps summary.
For 6200 AOS-CX 10.06, what is the syntax of the show erps summary command?
Syntax: show erps summary
For 6200 AOS-CX 10.06, how do you accessing the AOS-CX Web UI?

To accessing the AOS-CX Web UI, follow the documented procedure: 1. Start a supported web browser and enter the IP address of the management port in the address bar. Use 2. Optionally a pre- and post-login message may be displayed. The message can be customized or disabled 3. At the login page, enter your user name and password credentials, then click Login 4. After you log in, the main Web UI page is displayed.
For 6200 AOS-CX 10.06, how do you troubleshoot Web UI access issues?

To troubleshoot Web UI access issues, follow the documented procedure: 1. Log out from one of the existing sessions 2. If the session cookie is lost and it is not possible to log out of the session, then wait for the session idle.
For 6200 AOS-CX 10.06, what does the VSX command do?

The VSX command shows the Aruba Virtual Switching Framework (VSF) configuration and status information. This link is displayed for the 6200 and 6300 switches that support VSF. Not applicable to the 6100 Switch Series.
For 6200 AOS-CX 10.06, what does the IPv6 address command do?
The IPv6 address command sets the IPv6 address for a loopback interface. 14. Syntax: IPv6 address <IPv6-ADDR/MASK>.
For 6200 AOS-CX 10.06, what is the syntax of the show interface loopback command?
Syntax: show interface loopback [brief | instance <ID>]
For 6200 AOS-CX 10.06, what does the ip route command do?

The ip route command adds an IPv4 static route on the default VRF. The no form of this command deletes a IPv4 static route. Syntax: ip route <DEST-IPv4-ADDR>/<NETMASK> [<NEXT-HOP-IP-ADDR>|<INTERFACE>|reject|blackhole].
For 6200 AOS-CX 10.06, what is the syntax of the ip route command?
Syntax: ip route <DEST-IPv4-ADDR>/<NETMASK> [<NEXT-HOP-IP-ADDR>|<INTERFACE>|reject|blackhole]
For 6200 AOS-CX 10.06, what does the ip route distance command do?

The ip route distance command configures the administrative distance for the IPv4 static route. The no form of this command deletes the static route. Syntax: ip route <DEST-IPv4-ADDR>/<NETMASK> [<NEXT-HOP-IP-ADDR>|<INTERFACE>] distance <VALUE>.
For 6200 AOS-CX 10.06, what is the syntax of the ip route distance command?
Syntax: ip route <DEST-IPv4-ADDR>/<NETMASK> [<NEXT-HOP-IP-ADDR>|<INTERFACE>] distance <VALUE>
For 6200 AOS-CX 10.06, what does the ip route tag command do?
The ip route tag command configures tag for IPv4 static route. The no form of this command deletes tag for IPv4 static route. Syntax: ip route <A.B.C.D/M> [A.B.C.D | INTERFACE] tag <1-4294967295>.
For 6200 AOS-CX 10.06, what is the syntax of the ip route tag command?
Syntax: ip route <A.B.C.D/M> [A.B.C.D | INTERFACE] tag <1-4294967295>
For 6200 AOS-CX 10.06, what does the IPv6 route command do?

The IPv6 route command adds an IPv6 static route. The no form of this command deletes an IPv6 static route on the default VRF. Syntax: IPv6 route <DEST-IPv6-ADDR>/<MASK> [<NEXT-HOP-IP-ADDR>|<INTERFACE>|reject|blackhole].
For 6200 AOS-CX 10.06, what is the syntax of the IPv6 route command?
Syntax: IPv6 route <DEST-IPv6-ADDR>/<MASK> [<NEXT-HOP-IP-ADDR>|<INTERFACE>|reject|blackhole]
For 6200 AOS-CX 10.06, what does the IPv6 route tag command do?
The IPv6 route tag command configures tag for IPv6 static route. Syntax: IPv6 route <X:X::X:X/M> [X:X::X:X | INTERFACE] tag <1-4294967295>.
For 6200 AOS-CX 10.06, what is the syntax of the IPv6 route tag command?
Syntax: IPv6 route <X:X::X:X/M> [X:X::X:X | INTERFACE] tag <1-4294967295>

section: Configuring OSPF on the routing switch

messages.content: For 6200 AOS-CX 10.06, how do you configure OSPF on the routing switch?

To configure OSPF on the routing switch, follow the documented procedure: 1. Create the OSPF instance and enter the OSPF router configuration context using the following command 2. Configure a global router ID using the following command. For command details, see router-id. Commands shown in the procedure include: router ospf <PROCESS-ID>; router-id <ROUTER-ADDRESS>.

task: Configuring OSPF on the routing switch

messages.content: For 6200 AOS-CX 10.06, what does the area <AREA-ID> nssa [no-summary] command do?

The area <AREA-ID> nssa [no-summary] command sets the cost of the default route announced to NSSA or stub areas. The no form of this command resets the cost of the default route announced to NSSA or stub areas, to the default value of 1. Creates the NSSA area (Not So Stubby Area) with <AREA-ID> if not present. If area is present and not NSSA area, this command changes the area type to NSSA area. If no-summary is used, area type will be NSSA No- Summary. The no form of this command unsets the area type as NSSA. That is, the configured area will be changed to default normal area. The no area <AREA-ID> nssa no-summary command enables sending inter-area routes into NSSA, but will not unset the area as NSSA. Syntax: area <AREA-ID> nssa [no-summary].
For 6200 AOS-CX 10.06, what is the syntax of the area <AREA-ID> nssa [no-summary] command?
Syntax: area <AREA-ID> nssa [no-summary]
For 6200 AOS-CX 10.06, what does the clear ip ospf statistics command do?
The clear ip ospf statistics command clear the OSPF event statistics. Syntax: clear ip ospf [<PROCESS-ID>] statistics [interface [<INTERFACE-NAME>]] [all-vrfs | vrf <VRF-NAME>].
For 6200 AOS-CX 10.06, what is the syntax of the clear ip ospf statistics command?
Syntax: clear ip ospf [<PROCESS-ID>] statistics [interface [<INTERFACE-NAME>]] [all-vrfs | vrf <VRF-NAME>]
For 6200 AOS-CX 10.06, what does the default-information originate command do?

The default-information originate command configures OSPF to advertise the default route (0.0.0.0/0) to its neighbors if it is present in the routing table. The no form of this command disables advertisement of the default route. Configures OSPF to advertise the default route (0.0.0.0/0) to its neighbors, regardless if it is present in the routing table or not. The no form of this command sets the OSPF administrative distance to the default of 110. Syntax: default-information originate.
For 6200 AOS-CX 10.06, what is the syntax of the default-information originate command?
Syntax: default-information originate
For 6200 AOS-CX 10.06, what does the default-metric <METRIC-VALUE> command do?

The default-metric <METRIC-VALUE> command sets the default metric for redistributed routes in the OSPF. The no form of this command sets the default metric to be used for redistributed routes into OSPF to the default of 25. Disables the OSPF process. IMPORTANT: This command does not remove the OSPF configurations. Syntax: default-metric <METRIC-VALUE>.
For 6200 AOS-CX 10.06, what is the syntax of the default-metric <METRIC-VALUE> command?
Syntax: default-metric <METRIC-VALUE>
For 6200 AOS-CX 10.06, what does the distance <DISTANCE> command do?

The distance <DISTANCE> command defines an administrative distance for OSPF. Administrative distance is used as a criteria to select the best route when multiple routes are present from different routing protocols. The no form of this command sets the OSPF administrative distance to the default of 110. Enables the OSPF process, if disabled. By default the OSPF process is enabled. 46. Syntax: distance <DISTANCE>.
For 6200 AOS-CX 10.06, what is the syntax of the distance <DISTANCE> command?
Syntax: distance <DISTANCE>
For 6200 AOS-CX 10.06, what does the graceful-restart {restart-interval <SECONDS> | helper} command do?

The graceful-restart {restart-interval <SECONDS> | helper} command configures graceful restart parameters for OSPF. The no form of this command sets the restart interval to the default interval of 120 seconds or disables the helper mode, depending on the parameter specified. Syntax: graceful-restart {restart-interval <SECONDS> | helper}.
For 6200 AOS-CX 10.06, what is the syntax of the graceful-restart {restart-interval <SECONDS> | helper} command?
Syntax: graceful-restart {restart-interval <SECONDS> | helper}
For 6200 AOS-CX 10.06, what does the ip ospf area command do?

The ip ospf area command runs the OSPF protocol on the interface with the configured IPv4 address for the area specified. The interfaces which have an IP address configured in this network or in a subset of this network, will participate in the OSPF protocol. To move an interface to a new area, unmap the existing area and then associate a new area with the interface. The no form of this command disables OSPF on the interface and removes the interface from the area. Interfaces which have an IP address configured on the network or in a subset of the network, stop participating in the OSPF protocol. Syntax: ip ospf <PROCESS-ID> area <AREA-ID>.
For 6200 AOS-CX 10.06, what is the syntax of the ip ospf area command?
Syntax: ip ospf <PROCESS-ID> area <AREA-ID>
For 6200 AOS-CX 10.06, what does the ip ospf authentication command do?

The ip ospf authentication command sets the authentication type that will be used for authentication with the neighbor router. The no form of this command deletes the authentication type used for a particular authentication with the neighbor router and sets to null authentication. Syntax: ip ospf authentication {message-digest | simple-text | null | keychain}.

syntax: ip ospf authentication {message-digest | simple-text | null | keychain}

messages.content: For 6200 AOS-CX 10.06, what is the syntax of the ip ospf authentication command?
Syntax: ip ospf authentication {message-digest | simple-text | null | keychain}

syntax: ip ospf authentication {message-digest | simple-text | null | keychain}

messages.content: For 6200 AOS-CX 10.06, what does the ip ospf authentication-key command do?
The ip ospf authentication-key command is documented in this guide. Syntax: ip ospf authentication-key {ciphertext | plaintext} <PASSWORD>.
For 6200 AOS-CX 10.06, what is the syntax of the ip ospf authentication-key command?
Syntax: ip ospf authentication-key {ciphertext | plaintext} <PASSWORD>
For 6200 AOS-CX 10.06, what does the ip ospf cost command do?

The ip ospf cost command sets the cost (metric) associated with a particular interface. The interface cost is used as a parameter to calculate the best routes. The no form of this command sets the cost (metric) associated with a particular interface to the default cost 1. Syntax: ip ospf cost <INTERFACE-COST>.
For 6200 AOS-CX 10.06, what is the syntax of the ip ospf cost command?
Syntax: ip ospf cost <INTERFACE-COST>
For 6200 AOS-CX 10.06, what does the ip ospf dead-interval command do?

The ip ospf dead-interval command sets the interval after which a neighbor is declared dead if no hello packet is received on the OSPF interface. The no form of this command sets the interval after which a neighbor is declared dead, to the default for the OSPF interface. The default value is 40 seconds (generally 4 times the hello packet interval). Syntax: ip ospf dead-interval <INTERVAL>.
For 6200 AOS-CX 10.06, what is the syntax of the ip ospf dead-interval command?
Syntax: ip ospf dead-interval <INTERVAL>
For 6200 AOS-CX 10.06, what does the ip ospf hello-interval command do?

The ip ospf hello-interval command sets the time interval between OSPF hello packets for the OSPF interface. The no form of this command sets the time interval OSPF hello packets to the default of 10 seconds for the OSPF interface. Syntax: ip ospf hello-interval <INTERVAL>.
For 6200 AOS-CX 10.06, what is the syntax of the ip ospf hello-interval command?
Syntax: ip ospf hello-interval <INTERVAL>
For 6200 AOS-CX 10.06, what does the ip ospf keychain command do?

The ip ospf keychain command sets the key chain for md5 authentication. A key chain configures rotating keys for packet authenticating, reducing the risk of keys being compromised. The no form of this command deletes the key chain used for md5 authentication. Syntax: ip ospf keychain <KEYCHAIN-NAME>.
For 6200 AOS-CX 10.06, what is the syntax of the ip ospf keychain command?
Syntax: ip ospf keychain <KEYCHAIN-NAME>
For 6200 AOS-CX 10.06, what does the ip ospf network command do?

The ip ospf network command configures the OSPF network type for the interface. The no form of this command sets the network type for the interface to the system default which is broadcast network. Syntax: ip ospf network {broadcast | point-to-point}.
For 6200 AOS-CX 10.06, what is the syntax of the ip ospf network command?
Syntax: ip ospf network {broadcast | point-to-point}
For 6200 AOS-CX 10.06, what does the ip ospf passive command do?

The ip ospf passive command configures the interface as an OSPF passive interface. With this setting, the interface participates in OSPF but does not send or receive packets on that interface. The no form of this command resets the interface as active. With this setting, the interface starts sending and receiving OSPF packets. Syntax: ip ospf passive.
For 6200 AOS-CX 10.06, what is the syntax of the ip ospf passive command?
Syntax: ip ospf passive
For 6200 AOS-CX 10.06, what does the ip ospf priority command do?

The ip ospf priority command sets the OSPF priority for the interface. The larger the numeric value of the priority, the higher the chances for it to become the designated router. Setting a priority of zero makes the router ineligible to become a designated router or back up designated router. The no form of this command sets the OSPF priority for the interface to the default of 1. Syntax: ip ospf priority <PRIORITY-VALUE>.
For 6200 AOS-CX 10.06, what is the syntax of the ip ospf priority command?
Syntax: ip ospf priority <PRIORITY-VALUE>
For 6200 AOS-CX 10.06, what does the ip ospf retransmit-interval command do?

The ip ospf retransmit-interval command sets the time between retransmitting lost link state advertisements for the OSPF interface. The no form of this command sets the time between retransmitting lost link state advertisements to the default of 5 seconds for the OSPF interface. Syntax: ip ospf retransmit-interval <INTERVAL>.
For 6200 AOS-CX 10.06, what is the syntax of the ip ospf retransmit-interval command?
Syntax: ip ospf retransmit-interval <INTERVAL>
For 6200 AOS-CX 10.06, what does the ip ospf shutdown command do?

The ip ospf shutdown command disables OSPF on the interface. The interface state changes to Down. It does not remove the interface from the OSPF area. To remove the interface, use the command no ip ospf area. The no form of this command re-enables OSPF on the interface. Syntax: ip ospf shutdown.
For 6200 AOS-CX 10.06, what is the syntax of the ip ospf shutdown command?
Syntax: ip ospf shutdown
For 6200 AOS-CX 10.06, what does the ip ospf transit-delay command do?

The ip ospf transit-delay command sets the time delay in link state transmission for the OSPF interface. The no form of this command sets the delay in link state transmission to the default of 1 second for the OSPF interface. Syntax: ip ospf transit-delay <DELAY>.
For 6200 AOS-CX 10.06, what is the syntax of the ip ospf transit-delay command?
Syntax: ip ospf transit-delay <DELAY>
For 6200 AOS-CX 10.06, what does the keychain <KEYCHAIN-NAME> command do?

The keychain <KEYCHAIN-NAME> command sets the key chain for md5 authentication. A key chain configures rotating keys for packet authenticating, reducing the risk of keys being compromised. The no form of this command deletes the key chain used for md5 authentication. Syntax: keychain <KEYCHAIN-NAME>.
For 6200 AOS-CX 10.06, what is the syntax of the keychain <KEYCHAIN-NAME> command?
Syntax: keychain <KEYCHAIN-NAME>
For 6200 AOS-CX 10.06, what does the max-metric router-lsa [on-startup [<ADVERT-TIME>]] command do?

The max-metric router-lsa [on-startup [<ADVERT-TIME>]] command sets the protocol to advertise a maximum metric so that other routers do not prefer this router as an intermediate hop in their shortest path first (SPF) calculations. If the on-startup parameter is used, the router is configured to advertise a maximum metric at startup for the time mentioned in seconds or for a default value of 600 seconds. The no form of this command advertises the normal cost metrics instead of advertising the maximized cost metric. This setting causes the router to be considered in traffic forwarding. Configures all OSPF interfaces as passive. The no form of this command sets all OSPF interfaces as active. Syntax: max-metric router-lsa [on-startup [<ADVERT-TIME>]].
For 6200 AOS-CX 10.06, what is the syntax of the max-metric router-lsa [on-startup [<ADVERT-TIME>]] command?
Syntax: max-metric router-lsa [on-startup [<ADVERT-TIME>]]
For 6200 AOS-CX 10.06, what does the redistribute {bgp | connected | static | rip | ospf <PROCESS-ID>} [route-map <ROUTE-MAP-NAME>] command do?

The redistribute {bgp | connected | static | rip | ospf <PROCESS-ID>} [route-map <ROUTE-MAP-NAME>] command redistributes routes originating from other protocols, or from another OSPF process, to the current OSPF process. The no form of this command disables redistribution of routes to the current OSPF process. Syntax: redistribute {bgp | connected | static | rip | ospf <PROCESS-ID>} [route-map <ROUTE-MAP-NAME>].
For 6200 AOS-CX 10.06, what is the syntax of the redistribute {bgp | connected | static | rip | ospf <PROCESS-ID>} [route-map <ROUTE-MAP-NAME>] command?
Syntax: redistribute {bgp | connected | static | rip | ospf <PROCESS-ID>} [route-map <ROUTE-MAP-NAME>]
For 6200 AOS-CX 10.06, what does the reference-bandwidth <BANDWIDTH> command do?

The reference-bandwidth <BANDWIDTH> command sets the reference bandwidth for OSPFv2. If the OSPFv2 interface cost is not explicitly set. then the cost of all the OSPFv2 interfaces is recalculated based on the reference bandwidth and link speed of the interface. For VLAN interfaces the link speed value is taken as 1 Gbps (if the OSPFv2 interface cost is not explicitly set). The no form of this command sets the reference bandwidth for OSPF to the default of 100000 Mbps. Syntax: reference-bandwidth <BANDWIDTH>.
For 6200 AOS-CX 10.06, what is the syntax of the reference-bandwidth <BANDWIDTH> command?
Syntax: reference-bandwidth <BANDWIDTH>
For 6200 AOS-CX 10.06, what does the router ospf command do?

The router ospf command creates an OSPF process (if not created already) on a VRF, and switches to the OSPF router instance context. Up to eight OSPF processes are supported per VRF. The no form of this command removes the OSPF instance. Syntax: router ospf <PROCESS-ID> [vrf <VRF-NAME>].
For 6200 AOS-CX 10.06, what is the syntax of the router ospf command?
Syntax: router ospf <PROCESS-ID> [vrf <VRF-NAME>]
For 6200 AOS-CX 10.06, what does the router-id command do?

The router-id command sets an ID for the router in an IPv4 address format. The no form of this command unconfigures the router-id for the instance and sets the router-id to the default as follows: the router-id is selected dynamically as equal to the highest loopback address on the router, or the highest active interface if there are no loopback addresses. If no IP address is configured on any interfaces on the router, OSPF will not form an adjacency. Syntax: router-id <ROUTER-ADDR>.
For 6200 AOS-CX 10.06, what is the syntax of the router-id command?
Syntax: router-id <ROUTER-ADDR>
For 6200 AOS-CX 10.06, what is the syntax of the show ip ospf command?
Syntax: show ip ospf [<PROCESS-ID>] [all-vrfs | vrf <VRF-NAME>]
For 6200 AOS-CX 10.06, what does the show ip ospf interface command do?
The show ip ospf interface command displays general OSPF, area, state, and configuration information. Syntax: show ip ospf [<PROCESS-ID>] interface [<interface-name>] [brief].
For 6200 AOS-CX 10.06, what is the syntax of the show ip ospf interface command?
Syntax: show ip ospf [<PROCESS-ID>] interface [<interface-name>] [brief]
For 6200 AOS-CX 10.06, what does the show ip ospf lsdb command do?
The show ip ospf lsdb command is documented in this guide. Syntax: show ip ospf [<PROCESS-ID>] lsdb [all-vrfs | vrf <VRF-NAME>].
For 6200 AOS-CX 10.06, what is the syntax of the show ip ospf lsdb command?
Syntax: show ip ospf [<PROCESS-ID>] lsdb [all-vrfs | vrf <VRF-NAME>]
For 6200 AOS-CX 10.06, what does the show ip ospf routes command do?
The show ip ospf routes command is documented in this guide. Syntax: show ip ospf [<PROCESS-ID>] routes.
For 6200 AOS-CX 10.06, what is the syntax of the show ip ospf routes command?
Syntax: show ip ospf [<PROCESS-ID>] routes
For 6200 AOS-CX 10.06, what does the show ip ospf statistics command do?
The show ip ospf statistics command displays OSPF statistics. Syntax: show ip ospf [<PROCESS-ID>] statistics [all-vrfs | vrf <VRF-NAME>].
For 6200 AOS-CX 10.06, what is the syntax of the show ip ospf statistics command?
Syntax: show ip ospf [<PROCESS-ID>] statistics [all-vrfs | vrf <VRF-NAME>]
For 6200 AOS-CX 10.06, what does the summary-address <IPv4-ADDR>/<MASK> [no-advertise | tag <TAG-VALUE>] command do?

The summary-address <IPv4-ADDR>/<MASK> [no-advertise | tag <TAG-VALUE>] command summarizes the external routes with the matching address and mask. When advertising this route, its metric is set to the lowest cost path from among the routes that were summarized. The no form of this command disables route summarization. NOTE: This command only works for an ASBR (Autonomous System Boundary Router). Syntax: summary-address <IPv4-ADDR>/<MASK> [no-advertise | tag <TAG-VALUE>].
For 6200 AOS-CX 10.06, what is the syntax of the summary-address <IPv4-ADDR>/<MASK> [no-advertise | tag <TAG-VALUE>] command?
Syntax: summary-address <IPv4-ADDR>/<MASK> [no-advertise | tag <TAG-VALUE>]
For 6200 AOS-CX 10.06, what does the timers lsa-arrival <DELAY> command do?

The timers lsa-arrival <DELAY> command configures the minimum delay between receiving the same LSA from a peer. The same LSA is an LSA that contains the same LSA ID number, LSA type, and advertising router ID. If an instance of the same LSA arrives sooner before the delay expires, the LSA is dropped. Generally, the LSA arrival timer should be set to a value less than or equal to the start-time value for the command timers throttle lsa start on the neighbor. The no form of this command sets the LSA timers to default values. Syntax: timers lsa-arrival <DELAY>.
For 6200 AOS-CX 10.06, what is the syntax of the timers lsa-arrival <DELAY> command?
Syntax: timers lsa-arrival <DELAY>

section: timers throttle lsa start-time <START-TIME> hold-time <HOLD-TIME> max-wait-time <WAIT-TIME>

messages.content: For 6200 AOS-CX 10.06, what does the timers throttle lsa start-time <START-TIME> hold-time <HOLD-TIME> max-wait-time <WAIT-TIME> command do?

The timers throttle lsa start-time <START-TIME> hold-time <HOLD-TIME> max-wait-time <WAIT-TIME> command configures the timers for LSA generation. The no form of this command sets the LSA timers to default values. Configures timers for SPF calculation. There are three timers:. Syntax: timers throttle lsa start-time <START-TIME> hold-time <HOLD-TIME> max-wait-time <WAIT-TIME>.

command: timers throttle lsa start-time <START-TIME> hold-time <HOLD-TIME> max-wait-time <WAIT-TIME>
syntax: timers throttle lsa start-time <START-TIME> hold-time <HOLD-TIME> max-wait-time <WAIT-TIME>

messages.content: For 6200 AOS-CX 10.06, what is the syntax of the timers throttle lsa start-time <START-TIME> hold-time <HOLD-TIME> max-wait-time <WAIT-TIME> command?
Syntax: timers throttle lsa start-time <START-TIME> hold-time <HOLD-TIME> max-wait-time <WAIT-TIME>

command: timers throttle lsa start-time <START-TIME> hold-time <HOLD-TIME> max-wait-time <WAIT-TIME>
