section: Configuring external route redistribution and control

messages.content: For 6200 AOS-CX 10.06, how do you configure external route redistribution and control?

To configure external route redistribution and control, follow the documented procedure: 1. Enable route redistribution using the following command and specify one of the following parameters: 2. Optionally, modify the default metric for redistribution using the following command. For command 3. Optionally, set the cost of default-summary LSAs using the following command. For command details, .

task: Configuring external route redistribution and control

messages.content: For 6200 AOS-CX 10.06, what does the area <AREA-ID> command do?

The area <AREA-ID> command creates a normal area with <AREA-ID> set if not present. If area is present and is not the normal area, this command changes the area type to normal area. The no form of this command deletes the area with the <AREA-ID> specified. The area can be of any type (stub, stub no-summary, and default normal area). Syntax: area <AREA-ID>.
For 6200 AOS-CX 10.06, what is the syntax of the area <AREA-ID> command?
Syntax: area <AREA-ID>
For 6200 AOS-CX 10.06, what does the area <AREA-ID> authentication ipsec spi <SPI-INDEX> <AUTH-TYPE> <KEY-TYPE> <AUTH-KEY> command do?

The area <AREA-ID> authentication ipsec spi <SPI-INDEX> <AUTH-TYPE> <KEY-TYPE> <AUTH-KEY> command configures IPsec AH authentication for the specified area. OSPFv3 interfaces which have IPsec configured at the interface context will not use area level IPsec. The no form of this command removes IPsec AH authentication for the specified area. NOTE: IPsec is not supported for 6in6 tunnel interfaces. Syntax: area <AREA-ID> authentication ipsec spi <SPI-INDEX> <AUTH-TYPE> <KEY-TYPE> <AUTH-KEY>.
For 6200 AOS-CX 10.06, what is the syntax of the area <AREA-ID> authentication ipsec spi <SPI-INDEX> <AUTH-TYPE> <KEY-TYPE> <AUTH-KEY> command?
Syntax: area <AREA-ID> authentication ipsec spi <SPI-INDEX> <AUTH-TYPE> <KEY-TYPE> <AUTH-KEY>
For 6200 AOS-CX 10.06, what does the area <AREA-ID> encryption ipsec spi <SPI-INDEX> <AUTH-TYPE> <KEY-TYPE> <AUTH-KEY> command do?

The area <AREA-ID> encryption ipsec spi <SPI-INDEX> <AUTH-TYPE> <KEY-TYPE> <AUTH-KEY> command configures IPsec ESP with authentication/encryption algorithm type and key for the specified area. OSPFv3 interfaces with IPsec configured at interface context will not use area level IPsec ESP configuration. The no form of this command removes IPsec ESP from the specified area. NOTE: IPsec is not supported for 6in6 tunnel interfaces. Syntax: area <AREA-ID> encryption ipsec spi <SPI-INDEX> <AUTH-TYPE> <KEY-TYPE> <AUTH-KEY>.
For 6200 AOS-CX 10.06, what is the syntax of the area <AREA-ID> encryption ipsec spi <SPI-INDEX> <AUTH-TYPE> <KEY-TYPE> <AUTH-KEY> command?
Syntax: area <AREA-ID> encryption ipsec spi <SPI-INDEX> <AUTH-TYPE> <KEY-TYPE> <AUTH-KEY>
For 6200 AOS-CX 10.06, what does the clear IPv6 ospfv3 statistics command do?

The clear IPv6 ospfv3 statistics command clears the OSPFv3 event statistics. Sets the default metric for redistributed routes in the OSPFv3. Syntax: clear IPv6 ospfv3 [<PROCESS-ID>] statistics [interface [<INTERFACE-NAME>]].
For 6200 AOS-CX 10.06, what is the syntax of the clear IPv6 ospfv3 statistics command?
Syntax: clear IPv6 ospfv3 [<PROCESS-ID>] statistics [interface [<INTERFACE-NAME>]]
For 6200 AOS-CX 10.06, what does the disable command do?
The disable command disables the OSPFv3 process. By default OSPFv3 process is enabled. IMPORTANT: This command does not remove the OSPFv3 configurations. Syntax: disable.
For 6200 AOS-CX 10.06, what is the syntax of the disable command?
Syntax: disable
For 6200 AOS-CX 10.06, what does the graceful-restart {restart-interval <INTERVAL> | helper | strict-lsa-check} command do?

The graceful-restart {restart-interval <INTERVAL> | helper | strict-lsa-check} command configures graceful restart for OSPFv3. By default graceful restart is enabled on the OSPFv3 router. The no form of this command sets the restart interval to the default of 120 seconds or disables helper mode depending on the specified parameters. Syntax: graceful-restart {restart-interval <INTERVAL> | helper | strict-lsa-check}.
For 6200 AOS-CX 10.06, what is the syntax of the graceful-restart {restart-interval <INTERVAL> | helper | strict-lsa-check} command?
Syntax: graceful-restart {restart-interval <INTERVAL> | helper | strict-lsa-check}
For 6200 AOS-CX 10.06, what does the IPv6 ospfv3 area command do?

The IPv6 ospfv3 area command runs the OSPFv3 protocol on the interface for the area specified. To move an interface to a new area, unmap the existing area and then associate a new area with the interface. The no form of this command disables OSPF on the interface and removes the interface from the area. Interfaces which have an IP address configured on the network or in a subset of the network, stop participating in the OSPF protocol. Syntax: IPv6 ospfv3 <PROCESS-ID> area <AREA-ID>.
For 6200 AOS-CX 10.06, what is the syntax of the IPv6 ospfv3 area command?
Syntax: IPv6 ospfv3 <PROCESS-ID> area <AREA-ID>
For 6200 AOS-CX 10.06, what does the IPv6 ospfv3 authentication null command do?
The IPv6 ospfv3 authentication null command is documented in this guide. Syntax: IPv6 ospfv3 authentication null.
For 6200 AOS-CX 10.06, what is the syntax of the IPv6 ospfv3 authentication null command?
Syntax: IPv6 ospfv3 authentication null
For 6200 AOS-CX 10.06, what does the IPv6 ospfv3 authentication ipsec command do?

The IPv6 ospfv3 authentication ipsec command configures IPSec AH authentication. OSPFv3 interfaces that have IPsec configured at the interface context will not use area level IPsec. The no form of this command removes IPsec AH authentication for the specified area. Syntax: IPv6 ospfv3 authentication ipsec spi <SPI-INDEX> <AUTH-TYPE> <KEY-TYPE> <AUTH-KEY>.
For 6200 AOS-CX 10.06, what is the syntax of the IPv6 ospfv3 authentication ipsec command?
Syntax: IPv6 ospfv3 authentication ipsec spi <SPI-INDEX> <AUTH-TYPE> <KEY-TYPE> <AUTH-KEY>
For 6200 AOS-CX 10.06, what does the IPv6 ospfv3 cost command do?

The IPv6 ospfv3 cost command sets the cost (metric) associated with a particular interface. The interface cost is used as a parameter to calculate the best routes. The no form of this command sets the cost (metric) associated with a particular interface to the default of 1. Syntax: IPv6 ospfv3 cost <INTERFACE-COST>.
For 6200 AOS-CX 10.06, what is the syntax of the IPv6 ospfv3 cost command?
Syntax: IPv6 ospfv3 cost <INTERFACE-COST>
For 6200 AOS-CX 10.06, what does the IPv6 ospfv3 dead-interval command do?
The IPv6 ospfv3 dead-interval command is documented in this guide. Syntax: IPv6 ospfv3 dead-interval <INTERVAL>.
For 6200 AOS-CX 10.06, what is the syntax of the IPv6 ospfv3 dead-interval command?
Syntax: IPv6 ospfv3 dead-interval <INTERVAL>
For 6200 AOS-CX 10.06, what does the IPv6 ospfv3 encryption ipsec command do?

The IPv6 ospfv3 encryption ipsec command configures IPsec ESP. OSPFv3 interfaces that have IPsec configured at the interface context will not use area level IPsec ESP. The no form of this command removes IPsec ESP for the specified area. Syntax: IPv6 ospfv3 encryption ipsec spi <SPI-INDEX> <AUTH-TYPE> <KEY-TYPE> <AUTH-KEY>.
For 6200 AOS-CX 10.06, what is the syntax of the IPv6 ospfv3 encryption ipsec command?
Syntax: IPv6 ospfv3 encryption ipsec spi <SPI-INDEX> <AUTH-TYPE> <KEY-TYPE> <AUTH-KEY>
For 6200 AOS-CX 10.06, what does the IPv6 ospfv3 encryption null command do?
The IPv6 ospfv3 encryption null command configures NULL ESP on an interface which disables IPsec ESP. Syntax: IPv6 ospfv3 encryption null.
For 6200 AOS-CX 10.06, what is the syntax of the IPv6 ospfv3 encryption null command?
Syntax: IPv6 ospfv3 encryption null
For 6200 AOS-CX 10.06, what does the IPv6 ospfv3 hello-interval command do?

The IPv6 ospfv3 hello-interval command sets the time interval between OSPFv3 hello packets for the OSPFv3 interface. The no form of this command sets the time interval between OSPFv3 hello packets to the default for the OSPFv3 interface of 10 seconds. Syntax: IPv6 ospfv3 hello-interval <INTERVAL>.
For 6200 AOS-CX 10.06, what is the syntax of the IPv6 ospfv3 hello-interval command?
Syntax: IPv6 ospfv3 hello-interval <INTERVAL>
For 6200 AOS-CX 10.06, what does the IPv6 ospfv3 network command do?

The IPv6 ospfv3 network command configures the network type for the interface. By default the network type is broadcast network. The no form of this command sets the network type for the interface to the system default of broadcast network. Syntax: IPv6 ospfv3 network {broadcast|point-to-point}.
For 6200 AOS-CX 10.06, what is the syntax of the IPv6 ospfv3 network command?
Syntax: IPv6 ospfv3 network {broadcast|point-to-point}
For 6200 AOS-CX 10.06, what does the IPv6 ospfv3 passive command do?

The IPv6 ospfv3 passive command configures the interface as an OSPFv3 passive interface. With this setting, the interface participates in the OSPF, but does not send or receive OSPF packets on that interface. The no form of this command resets the interface as active. With this setting, the interface starts sending and receiving OSPF packets. Syntax: IPv6 ospfv3 passive.
For 6200 AOS-CX 10.06, what is the syntax of the IPv6 ospfv3 passive command?
Syntax: IPv6 ospfv3 passive
For 6200 AOS-CX 10.06, what does the IPv6 ospfv3 priority command do?

The IPv6 ospfv3 priority command sets the OSPFv3 priority for the interface. The larger the numeric value of the priority, the higher the chance it will become the designated router. Setting a priority of 0 makes the router ineligible to become a designated router or back up designated router. The no form of this command sets the OSPFv3 priority for the interface to the default of 1. Syntax: IPv6 ospfv3 priority <number-value>.
For 6200 AOS-CX 10.06, what is the syntax of the IPv6 ospfv3 priority command?
Syntax: IPv6 ospfv3 priority <number-value>
For 6200 AOS-CX 10.06, what does the IPv6 ospfv3 retransmit-interval command do?

The IPv6 ospfv3 retransmit-interval command sets the time between retransmitting lost link state advertisements for the OSPFv3 interface. The no form of this command sets the time between retransmitting lost link state advertisements to the default 5 seconds. Syntax: IPv6 ospfv3 retransmit-interval <INTERVAL>.
For 6200 AOS-CX 10.06, what is the syntax of the IPv6 ospfv3 retransmit-interval command?
Syntax: IPv6 ospfv3 retransmit-interval <INTERVAL>
For 6200 AOS-CX 10.06, what does the IPv6 ospfv3 shutdown command do?

The IPv6 ospfv3 shutdown command disables OSPFv3 on the interface. The interface state changes to Down. It does not remove the interface from the OSPF area. To remove the interface, use the command no ip ospf area. The no form of this command re-enables OSPFv3 on the interface. Syntax: IPv6 ospfv3 shutdown.
For 6200 AOS-CX 10.06, what is the syntax of the IPv6 ospfv3 shutdown command?
Syntax: IPv6 ospfv3 shutdown
For 6200 AOS-CX 10.06, what does the IPv6 ospfv3 transit-delay command do?

The IPv6 ospfv3 transit-delay command sets the time delay in Link state transmission for the OSPFv3 interface. The no form of this command sets the transit delay in Link state transmission to the default of 1 second. Sets the maximum number of ECMP routes that OSPFv3 can support. The no form of this command sets the maximum number of ECMP routes that OSPFv3 can support to the default value of 4. Syntax: IPv6 ospfv3 transit-delay <DELAY>.
For 6200 AOS-CX 10.06, what is the syntax of the IPv6 ospfv3 transit-delay command?
Syntax: IPv6 ospfv3 transit-delay <DELAY>
For 6200 AOS-CX 10.06, what does the max-metric router-lsa [on-startup <INTERVAL>] command do?

The max-metric router-lsa [on-startup <INTERVAL>] command sets the protocol to advertise a maximum metric so that other routers do not prefer the router as an intermediate hop in their shortest path first (SPF) calculations. If the on-startup parameter is used, the router is configured to advertise a maximum metric at startup. That is, for the time specified in seconds, or the default value of 600 seconds. To disable advertisement of the maximum metric, use the no form of the command. The no form of this command advertises the normal cost metrics instead of advertising the maximized cost metric. This setting causes the router to be considered in traffic forwarding. Syntax: max-metric router-lsa [on-startup <INTERVAL>].
For 6200 AOS-CX 10.06, what is the syntax of the max-metric router-lsa [on-startup <INTERVAL>] command?
Syntax: max-metric router-lsa [on-startup <INTERVAL>]
For 6200 AOS-CX 10.06, what does the passive-interface default command do?

The passive-interface default command sets all OSPFv3 interfaces as passive. The no form of this command sets all the OSPFv3 interfaces as active. Redistributes routes originating from other protocols, or from another OSPF process, to the current OSPF process. The no form of this command disables redistribution of routes to the current OSPF process. Syntax: passive-interface default.
For 6200 AOS-CX 10.06, what is the syntax of the passive-interface default command?
Syntax: passive-interface default
For 6200 AOS-CX 10.06, what does the retransmit-interval <INTERVAL> command do?

The retransmit-interval <INTERVAL> command sets the time between retransmitting lost link state advertisements for virtual links. The no form of this command sets the time between retransmitting lost link state advertisements to the default of 5 seconds for virtual links. Syntax: retransmit-interval <INTERVAL>.
For 6200 AOS-CX 10.06, what is the syntax of the retransmit-interval <INTERVAL> command?
Syntax: retransmit-interval <INTERVAL>
For 6200 AOS-CX 10.06, what does the router ospfv3 command do?

The router ospfv3 command creates the OSPFv3 process (if not created already) and enters the router OSPFv3 instance context. Optionally if specified, you can specify a named VRF, or the default VRF if the <vrf-name> is not specified. Only one OSPFv3 process is allowed per VRF. 116. Syntax: router ospfv3 <PROCESS-ID> [vrf <VRF-NAME>].
For 6200 AOS-CX 10.06, what is the syntax of the router ospfv3 command?
Syntax: router ospfv3 <PROCESS-ID> [vrf <VRF-NAME>]
For 6200 AOS-CX 10.06, what is the syntax of the show IPv6 ospfv3 command?
Syntax: show IPv6 ospfv3 [<PROCESS-ID>] [all-vrfs | vrf <VRF-NAME>]
For 6200 AOS-CX 10.06, what does the show IPv6 ospfv3 border-routers command do?

The show IPv6 ospfv3 border-routers command shows the OSPFv3 routing table entries for Area Border Router (ABR) and Autonomous System Border Router (ASBR). Syntax: show IPv6 ospfv3 [<PROCESS-ID>] border-routers [all-vrfs | vrf <VRF-NAME>].
For 6200 AOS-CX 10.06, what is the syntax of the show IPv6 ospfv3 border-routers command?
Syntax: show IPv6 ospfv3 [<PROCESS-ID>] border-routers [all-vrfs | vrf <VRF-NAME>]
For 6200 AOS-CX 10.06, what does the show IPv6 ospfv3 interface command do?
The show IPv6 ospfv3 interface command shows information about OSPFv3 enabled interfaces. Syntax: show IPv6 ospfv3 [<PROCESS-ID>] interface [<INTERFACE-NAME>] [brief].
For 6200 AOS-CX 10.06, what is the syntax of the show IPv6 ospfv3 interface command?
Syntax: show IPv6 ospfv3 [<PROCESS-ID>] interface [<INTERFACE-NAME>] [brief]
For 6200 AOS-CX 10.06, what does the show IPv6 ospfv3 neighbors command do?
The show IPv6 ospfv3 neighbors command shows information about OSPFv3 neighbors. Syntax: show IPv6 ospfv3 [<PROCESS-ID>] neighbors [<NEIGHBOR-ID>].
For 6200 AOS-CX 10.06, what is the syntax of the show IPv6 ospfv3 neighbors command?
Syntax: show IPv6 ospfv3 [<PROCESS-ID>] neighbors [<NEIGHBOR-ID>]
For 6200 AOS-CX 10.06, what does the show IPv6 ospfv3 routes command do?
The show IPv6 ospfv3 routes command shows the OSPFv3 routing table information. Syntax: show IPv6 ospfv3 [<PROCESS-ID>] routes [<PREFIX/LENGTH>].
For 6200 AOS-CX 10.06, what is the syntax of the show IPv6 ospfv3 routes command?
Syntax: show IPv6 ospfv3 [<PROCESS-ID>] routes [<PREFIX/LENGTH>]

The show IPv6 ospfv3 statistics command shows OSPFv3 statistics. Syntax: show IPv6 ospfv3 [<PROCESS-ID>] statistics.
For 6200 AOS-CX 10.06, what is the syntax of the show IPv6 ospfv3 statistics command?
Syntax: show IPv6 ospfv3 [<PROCESS-ID>] statistics
For 6200 AOS-CX 10.06, what does the show IPv6 ospfv3 statistics interface command do?
The show IPv6 ospfv3 statistics interface command shows the OSPFv3 statistics for the OSPFv3-enabled interfaces. Syntax: show IPv6 ospfv3 [<PROCESS-ID>] statistics interface [<INTERFACE-NAME>].
For 6200 AOS-CX 10.06, what is the syntax of the show IPv6 ospfv3 statistics interface command?
Syntax: show IPv6 ospfv3 [<PROCESS-ID>] statistics interface [<INTERFACE-NAME>]
For 6200 AOS-CX 10.06, what does the summary-address <IPv6-ADDR>/<MASK> [no-advertise | tag <TAG-VALUE>] command do?

The summary-address <IPv6-ADDR>/<MASK> [no-advertise | tag <TAG-VALUE>] command summarizes the external routes with the matching address and mask. When advertising this route, its metric is set to the lowest cost path from among the routes that were summarized. The no form of this command disables route summarization. NOTE: This command only works for an ASBR (Autonomous System Boundary Router). Syntax: summary-address <IPv6-ADDR>/<MASK> [no-advertise | tag <TAG-VALUE>].
For 6200 AOS-CX 10.06, what is the syntax of the summary-address <IPv6-ADDR>/<MASK> [no-advertise | tag <TAG-VALUE>] command?
Syntax: summary-address <IPv6-ADDR>/<MASK> [no-advertise | tag <TAG-VALUE>]
For 6200 AOS-CX 10.06, what does the timers throttle spf start-time <START-TIME> hold-time <HOLD-TINME> command do?

The timers throttle spf start-time <START-TIME> hold-time <HOLD-TINME> command configures timers for SPF calculation. There are three timers: • start-time Is the initial delay before an SPF calculation is started. Default is 200 milliseconds. • hold-time Is the progressive backoff time to wait before next scheduled SPF calculation. Default is 1000 milliseconds. If a route change event occurs during this period, the value doubles until it reaches the max-wait-time. • max-wait-time Is the maximum time to wait before the next scheduled SPF calculation. Default is 5000 milliseconds. This is used to limit the SPF hold timer and also defines the time to be considered for which the OSPF LSDB has to be stable, after which the SPF throttle mechanism is reset. The no form of this command sets all the configured non-default timers to default value. Syntax: timers throttle spf start-time <START-TIME> hold-time <HOLD-TINME>.
For 6200 AOS-CX 10.06, what is the syntax of the timers throttle spf start-time <START-TIME> hold-time <HOLD-TINME> command?
Syntax: timers throttle spf start-time <START-TIME> hold-time <HOLD-TINME>
For 6200 AOS-CX 10.06, what does the transit-delay <DELAY> command do?

The transit-delay <DELAY> command sets the time delay in Link state transmission for virtual links. The no form of this command sets the delay in Link state transmission to the default of 1 second for virtual links. Enables the notification of the events to be sent as traps to the SNMP management stations for OSPFv3. The no form of this command disables the notification of the events to be sent as traps to the SNMP management stations for OSPFv3. 130. Syntax: transit-delay <DELAY>.
For 6200 AOS-CX 10.06, what is the syntax of the transit-delay <DELAY> command?
Syntax: transit-delay <DELAY>

The accept-lifetime [start-time <time> <month>/<day>/<year>] {duration {<seconds> | infinite} | end-time <time> <month>/<day>/<year>} command configures the duration for which the key is valid for receiving packets. The no form of this command configures the key packet receiving duration to the default value of an infinite time. Syntax: accept-lifetime [start-time <time> <month>/<day>/<year>] {duration {<seconds> | infinite} | end-time <time> <month>/<day>/<year>}.

The key <key-id> command creates the key for a key chain and enters the key chain key context. A maximum of 64 keys can be configured per key chain. The no form of this command deletes the key from the key chain. Syntax: key <key-id>.

Syntax: send-lifetime [start-time <time> <month>/<day>/<year>] {duration {<seconds> | infinite} | end-time <time> <month>/<day>/<year>}.

messages.content: For 6200 AOS-CX 10.06, what does the client track ip { enable | disable | auto } command do?

The client track ip { enable | disable | auto } command enables client IP address tracking on the specified set of interfaces. Tracking will take effect only if client IP address tracking is enabled at both the system level and for the VLAN to which the port belongs. Default: auto. The no form of the command disables client IP address tracking on the specified set of interfaces. Configures the maximum number of clients to be tracked on the specified set of interfaces. For the 6100 Switch Series the no form of the command resets the client limit to the default value of 128. Syntax: client track ip { enable | disable | auto }.

messages.content: For 6200 AOS-CX 10.06, what does the ip rip command do?
The ip rip command enables RIP process on an interface. The no form of this command deletes the RIP process from an interface. Syntax: ip rip <PROCESS-ID> {all-ip | ip-address}.

For 6200 AOS-CX 10.06, what does the enable command do?

The enable command enables RIP process if disabled. By default RIP process is enabled. The no form of this command disables the RIP process. Disables RIP process. The no form of this command enables the RIP process. 150. Syntax: enable.

messages.content: For 6200 AOS-CX 10.06, what does the router ripng command do?

The router ripng command creates RIPng process if not already created and enters the router ripng <PROCESS-ID> context for the VRF mentioned. If no VRF is mentioned, a default is used. Only one RIPng process is allowed per VRF. The no form of this command deletes the RIPng instance for the VRF. If no VRF is mentioned the default is deleted. Syntax: router ripng <PROCESS-ID> [vrf <VRF-NAME>].

messages.content: For 6200 AOS-CX 10.06, what is IRDP?

IRDP: ICMP Router Discovery Protocol (IRDP), an extension of the ICMP, is independent of any routing protocol. It allows hosts to discover the IP addresses of neighboring routers that can act as default gateways to reach devices on other IP networks. IRDP operation IRDP uses the following types of ICMP messages: • Router advertisement (RA): Sent by a router to advertise IP addresses (including the primary and secondary IP addresses) and preference. • Router solicitation (RS): Sent by a host to request the IP addresses of routers on the subnet. An interface with IRDP enabled periodically broadcasts or multicasts an RA message to advertise its IP addresses. A receiving host adds the IP addresses to its routing table, and selects the IP address with the highest preference as the default gateway. When a host attached to the subnet starts up, the host multicasts an RS message to request immediate advertisements. If the host does not receive any advertisements, it retransmits the RS several times.
For 6200 AOS-CX 10.06, how do you configure IRDP?

To configure IRDP, follow the documented procedure: 1. Enable IRDP on an interface with the command ip irdp 2. Set the maximum hold time with the command ip irdp holdtime 3. Set the maximum router advertisement interval with the command ip irdp maxadvertinterval 4. Set the minimum router advertisement interval with the command ip irdp minadvertinterval 5. Set the IRDP preference level with the command ip irdp preference 6. Review IRDP configuration settings with the command show ip irdp.
For 6200 AOS-CX 10.06, what does the diag-dump irdp basic command do?
The diag-dump irdp basic command displays diagnostic information for IRDP. Syntax: diag-dump irdp basic.
For 6200 AOS-CX 10.06, what is the syntax of the diag-dump irdp basic command?
Syntax: diag-dump irdp basic
For 6200 AOS-CX 10.06, what does the ip irdp command do?

The ip irdp command enables IRDP on an interface and specifies the packet type that is used to send advertisements. By default, the packet type is set to multicast. IRDP is only supported on layer 3 interfaces. The no form of this command disables IRDP on an interface. Syntax: ip irdp [broadcast | multicast].
For 6200 AOS-CX 10.06, what is the syntax of the ip irdp command?
Syntax: ip irdp [broadcast | multicast]
For 6200 AOS-CX 10.06, what does the ip irdp holdtime command do?

The ip irdp holdtime command specifies the maximum amount of time the host will consider an advertisement to be valid until a newer advertisement arrives. When a new advertisement arrives, hold time is reset. Hold time must be greater than or equal to the maximum advertisement interval. Therefore, if the hold time for an advertisement expires, the host can reasonably conclude that the router interface that sent the advertisement is no longer available. The default hold time is three times the maximum advertisement interval. Syntax: ip irdp holdtime <TIME>.
For 6200 AOS-CX 10.06, what is the syntax of the ip irdp holdtime command?
Syntax: ip irdp holdtime <TIME>
For 6200 AOS-CX 10.06, what does the ip irdp maxadvertinterval command do?
The ip irdp maxadvertinterval command specifies the maximum router advertisement interval. Syntax: ip irdp maxadvertinterval <TIME>.
For 6200 AOS-CX 10.06, what is the syntax of the ip irdp maxadvertinterval command?
Syntax: ip irdp maxadvertinterval <TIME>
For 6200 AOS-CX 10.06, what does the ip irdp minadvertinterval command do?

The ip irdp minadvertinterval command specifies the minimum amount of time the switch waits between sending router advertisements. By default, this value is automatically set by the switch to be 75% of the value configured for maximum router advertisement interval. Use this command to override the automatically configured value. Syntax: ip irdp minadvertinterval <TIME>.
For 6200 AOS-CX 10.06, what is the syntax of the ip irdp minadvertinterval command?
Syntax: ip irdp minadvertinterval <TIME>
For 6200 AOS-CX 10.06, what does the ip irdp preference command do?

The ip irdp preference command specifies the IRDP preference level. If a host receives multiple router advertisement messages from different routers, the host selects the router that sent the message with the highest preference as the default gateway. Syntax: ip irdp preference <LEVEL>.
For 6200 AOS-CX 10.06, what is the syntax of the ip irdp preference command?
Syntax: ip irdp preference <LEVEL>
For 6200 AOS-CX 10.06, what does the show ip irdp command do?
The show ip irdp command displays IRDP configuration settings. Syntax: show ip irdp [vsx-peer].
For 6200 AOS-CX 10.06, what is the syntax of the show ip irdp command?
Syntax: show ip irdp [vsx-peer]
For 6200 AOS-CX 10.06, what does the guide say about IPv6 Router Advertisement?

IPv6 Router Advertisement: IPv6 RA provides a method for local IPv6 hosts to automatically configure their own IP address (and other settings such as a preferred DNS server) based on information advertised by switches/routers operating on the network. IPv6 flags Behavior of IPv6 hosts to IPv6 RA messages is controlled by the managed address configuration flag (M flag), and other stateful configuration flag (O flag). M flag O flag Description 0 0 1 1 0 1 0 1 Indicates that no information is available via DHCPv6. Indicates that other configuration information is available via DHCPv6. Examples of such information are DNS-related information or information on other servers within the network. Indicates that addresses are available via Dynamic Host Configuration Protocol (DHCPv6). If the M flag is set, the O flag is redundant and can be ignored because DHCPv6 will return all available configuration information.
For 6200 AOS-CX 10.06, how do you configure IPv6 RA?

To configure IPv6 RA, follow the documented procedure: 1. Enable transmission of IPv6 router advertisements with the command no IPv6 nd suppress-ra 2. Optionally, configure IPv6 unicast address prefixes with the command IPv6 nd prefix 3. Optionally, configure support for DNS name resolution with the commands IPv6 nd ra dns server.
For 6200 AOS-CX 10.06, what does the IPv6 address autoconfig command do?

The IPv6 address autoconfig command enables the interface to automatically obtain an IPv6 address using router advertisement information and the EUI-64 identifier. The no form of this command disables address auto-configuration. 20 AOS-CX 10.06 IP Services Guide NOTE: • A maximum of 15 autoconfigured addresses are supported. • This command automatically creates an IPv6 link-local address on the interface. However, it does not add the IPv6 address link-local command to the running configuration. If you remove the IPv6 address, the link-local address is also removed. To maintain the link- local address, you must manually execute the IPv6 address link-local command. Syntax: IPv6 address autoconfig.
For 6200 AOS-CX 10.06, what is the syntax of the IPv6 address autoconfig command?
Syntax: IPv6 address autoconfig
For 6200 AOS-CX 10.06, what does the IPv6 address link-local command do?

The IPv6 address link-local command enables IPv6 on the current interface. If no address is specified, an IPv6 link-local address is auto-generated for the interface. If an address is specified, auto-configuration is disabled and the specified address/mask is assigned to the interface. To disable IPv6 link-local on the interface, remove IPv6 address link-local, IPv6 address <global- IPv6-address>, and IPv6 address autoconfig from the interface. NOTE: This feature is not available on the management VRF. Syntax: IPv6 address link-local [<IPv6-ADDR>/<MASK>].
For 6200 AOS-CX 10.06, what is the syntax of the IPv6 address link-local command?
Syntax: IPv6 address link-local [<IPv6-ADDR>/<MASK>]
For 6200 AOS-CX 10.06, what does the IPv6 nd cache-limit command do?

The IPv6 nd cache-limit command configures the limit on the number of neighbor entries in the ND cache. The no form of this command sets the cache limit to the default value. Syntax: IPv6 nd cache-limit <CACHELIMIT>.
For 6200 AOS-CX 10.06, what is the syntax of the IPv6 nd cache-limit command?
Syntax: IPv6 nd cache-limit <CACHELIMIT>
For 6200 AOS-CX 10.06, what does the IPv6 nd dad attempts command do?

The IPv6 nd dad attempts command configures the number of neighbor solicitations to be sent when performing duplicate address detection (DAD) for a unicast address configured on an interface. The no form of this command sets the number of attempts to the default value. Syntax: IPv6 nd dad attempts <NUM-ATTEMPTS>.
For 6200 AOS-CX 10.06, what is the syntax of the IPv6 nd dad attempts command?
Syntax: IPv6 nd dad attempts <NUM-ATTEMPTS>
For 6200 AOS-CX 10.06, what does the IPv6 nd hop-limit command do?

The IPv6 nd hop-limit command configures the hop limit to be sent in RAs. The no form of this command resets the hop limit to 0. This reset eliminates the hop limit from the RAs that originate on the interface, so the host determines the hop limit. Syntax: IPv6 nd hop-limit <HOPLIMIT>.
For 6200 AOS-CX 10.06, what is the syntax of the IPv6 nd hop-limit command?
Syntax: IPv6 nd hop-limit <HOPLIMIT>
For 6200 AOS-CX 10.06, what does the IPv6 nd mtu command do?
The IPv6 nd mtu command configures the MTU size to be sent in the RA messages. The no form of this command sets hop limit to the default value. Syntax: IPv6 nd mtu <MTU-VALUE>.
For 6200 AOS-CX 10.06, what is the syntax of the IPv6 nd mtu command?
Syntax: IPv6 nd mtu <MTU-VALUE>
For 6200 AOS-CX 10.06, what does the IPv6 nd ns-interval command do?

The IPv6 nd ns-interval command configures the ND time between DAD neighbor solicitations sent for an unresolved destination, or between duplicate address detection neighbor solicitation requests. Increase this setting when neighbor solicitation retries or failures are occurring, or in a slow (WAN) network. The no form of this command sets the ns-interval to the default value. Syntax: IPv6 nd ns-interval <TIME>.
For 6200 AOS-CX 10.06, what is the syntax of the IPv6 nd ns-interval command?
Syntax: IPv6 nd ns-interval <TIME>
For 6200 AOS-CX 10.06, what does the IPv6 nd prefix command do?

The IPv6 nd prefix command specifies prefixes for the routing switch to include in RAs transmitted on the interface. IPv6 hosts use the prefixes in RAs to autoconfigure themselves with global unicast addresses. The autoconfigured address of a host is composed of the advertised prefix and the interface identifier in the current link-local address of the host. By default, advertise, autoconfig, and onlink are set. The no form of this command removes the configuration on the interface. Syntax: IPv6 nd prefix <IPv6-ADDR>/<PREFIX-LEN>.
For 6200 AOS-CX 10.06, what is the syntax of the IPv6 nd prefix command?
Syntax: IPv6 nd prefix <IPv6-ADDR>/<PREFIX-LEN>
For 6200 AOS-CX 10.06, what does the IPv6 nd ra dns search-list command do?

The IPv6 nd ra dns search-list command configures the DNS Search List (DNSSL) to include in Router Advertisements (RAs) transmitted on the interface. The no form of this command removes the DNS Search List from the RAs transmitted on the interface. Syntax: IPv6 nd ra dns search-list <DOMAIN-NAME> [lifetime <TIME>].
For 6200 AOS-CX 10.06, what is the syntax of the IPv6 nd ra dns search-list command?
Syntax: IPv6 nd ra dns search-list <DOMAIN-NAME> [lifetime <TIME>]
For 6200 AOS-CX 10.06, what does the IPv6 nd ra dns server command do?

The IPv6 nd ra dns server command configures the IPv6 address of a preferred Recursive DNS Server (RDNSS) to be included in Router Advertisements (RAs) transmitted on the interface. The no form of this command removes the configured DNS server from the RAs transmitted on the interface. Syntax: IPv6 nd ra dns server <IPv6-ADDR> [lifetime <TIME>].
For 6200 AOS-CX 10.06, what is the syntax of the IPv6 nd ra dns server command?
Syntax: IPv6 nd ra dns server <IPv6-ADDR> [lifetime <TIME>]
For 6200 AOS-CX 10.06, what does the IPv6 nd ra lifetime command do?

The IPv6 nd ra lifetime command configures the lifetime, in seconds, for the routing switch to be used as a default router by hosts on the current interface. The no form of this command sets lifetime to the default of 1800 seconds. Syntax: IPv6 nd ra lifetime <TIME>.
For 6200 AOS-CX 10.06, what is the syntax of the IPv6 nd ra lifetime command?
Syntax: IPv6 nd ra lifetime <TIME>
For 6200 AOS-CX 10.06, what does the IPv6 nd ra managed-config-flag command do?

The IPv6 nd ra managed-config-flag command controls the M fLAGsetting in RAs the router transmits on the current interface. Enable the M flag to indicate that hosts can obtain IP address through DHCPv6. The M flag is disabled by default. The no form of this command turns off (disables) the M flag. Syntax: IPv6 nd ra managed-config-flag.
For 6200 AOS-CX 10.06, what is the syntax of the IPv6 nd ra managed-config-flag command?
Syntax: IPv6 nd ra managed-config-flag
For 6200 AOS-CX 10.06, what does the IPv6 nd ra min-interval command do?

The IPv6 nd ra min-interval command configures the minimum interval between transmissions of IPv6 RAs on the interface. The interval between RA transmissions on an interface is a random value that changes every time an RA is sent. The interval is calculated to be a value between the current max-interval and min-interval settings. The no form of this command returns the setting to its default, provided the default value is less than the current max-interval setting. Syntax: IPv6 nd ra min-interval <TIME>.
For 6200 AOS-CX 10.06, what is the syntax of the IPv6 nd ra min-interval command?
Syntax: IPv6 nd ra min-interval <TIME>
For 6200 AOS-CX 10.06, what does the IPv6 nd ra other-config-flag command do?

The IPv6 nd ra other-config-flag command controls the O-bit in RAs the router transmits on the current interface; but is ignored unless the M-bit is disabled in RAs. Configure to set the O-bit in RA messages for host to obtain network parameters through DHCPv6. The other-config-flag is disabled by default. For more information on configuring the M-bit, see IPv6 nd ra managed-config-flag. The no form of this command turns off (disables) the setting for this command in RAs. Syntax: IPv6 nd ra other-config-flag.
For 6200 AOS-CX 10.06, what is the syntax of the IPv6 nd ra other-config-flag command?
Syntax: IPv6 nd ra other-config-flag
For 6200 AOS-CX 10.06, what does the IPv6 nd ra reachable-time command do?

The IPv6 nd ra reachable-time command sets the amount of time that the interface considers a device to be reachable after receiving a reachability confirmation from the device. The no form of this command sets the reachable time to the default value of 0. (no limit). Syntax: IPv6 nd ra reachable-time <TIME>.
For 6200 AOS-CX 10.06, what is the syntax of the IPv6 nd ra reachable-time command?
Syntax: IPv6 nd ra reachable-time <TIME>
For 6200 AOS-CX 10.06, what does the IPv6 nd ra retrans-timer command do?

The IPv6 nd ra retrans-timer command configures the period (retransmit timer) between ND solicitations sent by a host for an unresolved destination, or between DAD neighbor solicitation requests. By default, hosts on the interface use their own locally configured NS-interval settings instead of using the value received in the RAs. Increase this timer when neighbor solicitation retries or failures are occur, or in a "slow" (WAN) network. The no form of this command sets the value to the default of 0. Syntax: IPv6 nd ra retrans-timer <TIME>.
For 6200 AOS-CX 10.06, what is the syntax of the IPv6 nd ra retrans-timer command?
Syntax: IPv6 nd ra retrans-timer <TIME>
For 6200 AOS-CX 10.06, what does the IPv6 nd router-preference command do?

The IPv6 nd router-preference command specifies the value that is set in the Default Router Preference (DRP) field of Router Advertisements (RAs) that the switch sends from an interface. An interface with a DRP value of high will be preferred by other devices on the network over interfaces with an RA value of medium or low. The no form of this command set the value to the default of medium. Syntax: IPv6 nd router-preference {high | medium | low}.
For 6200 AOS-CX 10.06, what is the syntax of the IPv6 nd router-preference command?
Syntax: IPv6 nd router-preference {high | medium | low}
For 6200 AOS-CX 10.06, what does the IPv6 nd suppress-ra command do?
The IPv6 nd suppress-ra command configures suppression of IPv6 Router Advertisement transmissions on an interface. Syntax: IPv6 nd suppress-ra [<SUPPRESS-OPTION>].
For 6200 AOS-CX 10.06, what is the syntax of the IPv6 nd suppress-ra command?
Syntax: IPv6 nd suppress-ra [<SUPPRESS-OPTION>]
For 6200 AOS-CX 10.06, what does the show IPv6 nd global traffic command do?
The show IPv6 nd global traffic command displays IPv6 Neighbor Discovery traffic details on a device. Syntax: show IPv6 nd global traffic [vsx-peer].
For 6200 AOS-CX 10.06, what is the syntax of the show IPv6 nd global traffic command?
Syntax: show IPv6 nd global traffic [vsx-peer]
For 6200 AOS-CX 10.06, what does the show IPv6 nd interface command do?

The show IPv6 nd interface command displays neighbor discovery information for an interface. If no options are specified, displays information for the default VRF. Syntax: show IPv6 nd interface [<IF-NAME> | all-vrfs | vrf <VRF-NAME>].

syntax: show IPv6 nd interface [<IF-NAME> | all-vrfs | vrf <VRF-NAME>]

messages.content: For 6200 AOS-CX 10.06, what is the syntax of the show IPv6 nd interface command?
Syntax: show IPv6 nd interface [<IF-NAME> | all-vrfs | vrf <VRF-NAME>]

syntax: show IPv6 nd interface [<IF-NAME> | all-vrfs | vrf <VRF-NAME>]

The show IPv6 nd ra dns search-list command displays domain name information on all interfaces. Syntax: show IPv6 nd ra dns search-list [vsx-peer].
